light_analysis.motivation: 构建可信赖的大型语言模型需要其具备不确定性表达能力，但现有RL范式（如GRPO）因二元决策空间和静态不确定性奖励存在优势偏差，导致模型要么过度保守要么过度自信，尤其在关键应用中产生错误。需要一种机制来平衡确定性与不确定性，避免奖励破解和过度自信。
light_analysis.method: UCPO框架包括两个核心机制：三元优势解耦，将确定性轨迹和不确定性轨迹分离并独立归一化，消除优势偏差；动态不确定性奖励调整，根据模型演化和实例难度实时调整不确定性权重。实验在数学推理和通用任务上验证。
light_analysis.result: 在数学推理和通用任务实验中，UCPO有效解决了奖励不平衡问题，显著提升了模型在知识边界之外的可靠性。具体性能指标摘要未明确说明，但与基线GRPO相比，优势偏差得到缓解，模型更加稳健。
light_analysis.conclusion: UCPO通过解决优势偏差和动态调整不确定性奖励，提升了LLMs的不确定性表达能力，增强了模型在关键任务中的可信赖性。未来可应用于更多高安全需求场景，但需进一步探索计算开销和规模扩展。

title: Graph is a Substrate Across Data Modalities
abstract: Graphs provide a natural representation of relational structure that arises across diverse domains. Despite this ubiquity, graph structure is typically learned in a modality- and task-isolated manner, where graph representations are constructed within individual task contexts and discarded thereafter. As a result, structural regularities across modalities and tasks are repeatedly reconstructed rather than accumulated at the level of intermediate graph representations. This motivates a representation-learning question: how should graph structure be organized so that it can persist and accumulate across heterogeneous modalities and tasks? We adopt a representation-centric perspective in which graph structure is treated as a structural substrate that persists across learning contexts. To instantiate this perspective, we propose G-Substrate, a graph substrate framework that organizes learning around shared graph structures. G-Substrate comprises two complementary mechanisms: a unified structural schema that ensures compatibility among graph representations across heterogeneous modalities and tasks, and an interleaved role-based training strategy that exposes the same graph structure to multiple functional roles during learning. Experiments across multiple domains, modalities, and tasks show that G-Substrate outperforms task-isolated and naive multi-task learning methods. The codebase, model, and datasets are available at https://github.com/zmli6/G-Substrate.
comment: Graph structure across data modalities, accepted by ICML26

light_analysis.overview: 提出G-Substrate框架，将图结构作为跨模态和任务的共享基质，实现结构积累与复用。
light_analysis.motivation: 图结构虽广泛存在于不同领域，但当前方法通常以模态和任务孤立方式学习，导致跨模态和任务的结构规律被重复重建而非积累。这阻碍了图表示学习的通用性和效率，因此需要一种组织图结构使其能跨异构模态和任务持久积累的方法。
light_analysis.method: G-Substrate包含两个互补机制：统一结构模式确保跨异构模态和任务的图表示兼容性，以及交错角色训练策略使同一图结构在学习过程中暴露于多种功能角色。框架围绕共享图结构组织学习，支持多模态多任务协同训练。
light_analysis.result: 实验在多个领域、模态和任务上验证，G-Substrate优于任务孤立学习和朴素多任务学习方法。具体性能指标摘要未明确给出，但结果表明其有效利用跨任务结构共性，提升了泛化能力。
light_analysis.conclusion: G-Substrate通过将图结构视为持久基质，实现了跨模态和任务的结构积累与共享，促进了图表示学习的通用性。未来可探索更复杂的结构模式，并应用于更多异构场景。局限性包括对大规模图的计算开销需进一步优化。

title: Demystifying Mergeability: Interpretable Properties to Predict Model Merging Success
abstract: Model merging combines knowledge from separately fine-tuned models, yet the factors driving its success remain poorly understood. While recent work treats mergeability as an intrinsic property of the models, we show with an architecture-agnostic framework that it fundamentally depends on both the merging method and the partner tasks. Using L1-regularized linear optimization over a set of interpretable pairwise metrics (e.g., gradient L_2 distance), we uncover properties correlating with post-merge normalized accuracy across five merging methods. We find architecture- and method-specific variation in success drivers (64.0% average top-5 metric overlap; 79.3% sign agreement), with certain methods, notably TIES, exhibiting distinct ``fingerprints'' that diverge from the broader consensus. Crucially, however, gradient alignment metrics consistently emerge as the most fundamental signals of compatibility. These findings provide a diagnostic foundation for understanding mergeability and motivate future merge-aware fine-tuning strategies.
comment: 9 pages of main paper, 3 figures in the main paper, 4 tables in the main paper, many more figures and tables in the appendix

light_analysis.overview: 提出架构无关框架，用可解释配对度量预测模型合并成功，发现梯度对齐是关键信号。
light_analysis.motivation: 模型合并结合不同微调模型的知识，但成功因素不明。现有工作将可合并性视为模型固有属性，本研究指出其同时依赖于合并方法和任务对，需要揭示可解释的预测属性以提高合并的可控性和效率。
light_analysis.method: 采用L1正则化线性优化，从一组可解释的配对度量（如梯度L2距离）中筛选与五种合并方法后归一化精度相关的属性。框架架构无关，通过分析前5重要指标的重叠率和符号一致性，识别方法特有的“指纹”。
light_analysis.result: 平均前5指标重叠64.0%，符号一致性79.3%。TIES方法展现独特“指纹”，与其他方法共识相异。梯度对齐度量（如梯度L2距离）在所有方法中均为最基础的兼容性信号，优于其他度量。
light_analysis.conclusion: 揭示了合并方法、任务伙伴与可合并性的非固有依赖关系，为理解模型合并提供诊断基础。梯度对齐的重要性启发未来合并感知的微调策略，但研究限于特定方法组合，需扩展至更多方法和场景。

title: Learning Decentralized LLM Collaboration with Multi-Agent Actor Critic
abstract: Recent work has explored optimizing LLM collaboration through Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). However, most MARL fine-tuning approaches rely on predefined execution protocols, which often require centralized execution. Decentralized LLM collaboration is more appealing in practice, as agents can run inference in parallel with flexible deployments. Also, current approaches use Monte Carlo methods for fine-tuning, which suffer from high variance and thus require more samples to train effectively. Actor-critic methods are prevalent in MARL for dealing with these issues; thus, we developed Multi-Agent Actor-Critic (MAAC) methods to optimize decentralized LLM collaboration. In this paper, we analyze when and why these MAAC methods are beneficial. We propose 2 MAAC approaches, \textbf{CoLLM-CC} with a \textbf{C}entralized \textbf{C}ritic and \textbf{CoLLM-DC} with \textbf{D}ecentralized \textbf{C}ritics. Our experiments across writing, coding, and game-playing domains show that Monte Carlo methods and CoLLM-DC can achieve performance comparable to CoLLM-CC in short-horizon and dense-reward settings. However, they both underperform CoLLM-CC on long-horizon or sparse-reward tasks, where Monte Carlo methods require substantially more samples and CoLLM-DC struggles to converge.

light_analysis.overview: 提出两种基于多智能体Actor-Critic的去中心化LLM协作方法CoLLM-CC和CoLLM-DC，并分析其适用场景。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有LLM协作优化多采用多智能体强化学习，但大多依赖预定义执行协议和集中式执行，难以实现灵活并行的去中心化部署。同时，蒙特卡洛方法因高方差需要大量样本，而Actor-Critic方法可缓解此问题。因此研究提出去中心化协作的Actor-Critic方法，并分析其优势与局限。
light_analysis.method: 提出两种多智能体Actor-Critic方法：CoLLM-CC采用集中式评论家，CoLLM-DC采用去中心化评论家。通过分析两种方法在不同任务类型（短视界、密集奖励 vs. 长视界、稀疏奖励）中的表现，揭示其适用条件。实验涵盖写作、编码和游戏领域。
light_analysis.result: 在短视界、密集奖励任务中，蒙特卡洛方法和CoLLM-DC性能接近CoLLM-CC。但在长视界、稀疏奖励任务中，蒙特卡洛方法需要更多样本，CoLLM-DC难以收敛，两者均不如CoLLM-CC。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文贡献在于提出了两种去中心化LLM协作的Actor-Critic方法，并系统分析了不同方法在多种任务中的表现差异。研究揭示了集中式评论家在长视界、稀疏奖励场景下的优势，为LLM协作的实践部署提供了指导。未来工作可探索更高效的去中心化协作机制。

title: Constrained Meta Reinforcement Learning with Provable Test-Time Safety
abstract: Meta reinforcement learning (RL) allows agents to leverage experience across a distribution of tasks on which the agent can train at will, enabling faster learning of optimal policies on new test tasks. Despite its success in improving sample complexity on test tasks, many real-world applications, such as robotics and healthcare, impose safety constraints during testing. Constrained meta RL provides a promising framework for integrating safety into meta RL. An open question in constrained meta RL is how to ensure safety of the policy on the real-world test task, while reducing the sample complexity and thus, enabling faster learning of optimal policies. To address this gap, we propose an algorithm that refines policies learned during training, with provable safety and sample complexity guarantees for learning a near optimal policy on the test tasks. We further derive a matching lower bound, showing that this sample complexity is tight.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种带可证明测试时安全保证的约束元强化学习算法，并给出紧的样本复杂度下界。
light_analysis.motivation: 元强化学习在测试时需满足安全约束，但现有方法无法保证策略在真实世界测试任务中的安全性。实际应用如机器人和医疗领域，安全至关重要。因此，亟需一种既能降低样本复杂度、加速最优策略学习，又能提供可证明安全保证的方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出一种算法，在训练后对策略进行精细化调整，同时提供可证明的安全性和样本复杂度保证，确保在测试任务上学到近最优策略。进一步推导了匹配的下界，证明样本复杂度的紧致性。算法基于约束元强化学习框架，未明确提及具体模型或数据集。
light_analysis.result: 论文未给出具体实验数据，但提供了理论保证：所提算法能保证测试时的安全性，并具有最优的样本复杂度（与下界匹配），表明在安全性与学习效率之间取得了理论最优平衡。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究解决了约束元强化学习中测试时安全性的核心难题，提供了可证明的安全保证和紧的样本复杂度边界，为安全关键应用（如机器人、医疗）中的元学习提供了理论基础。未来可探索扩展到更复杂的安全约束或实际系统验证。

title: ECSEL: Explainable Classification via Signomial Equation Learning
abstract: We introduce ECSEL, an explainable classification method that learns formal expressions in the form of signomial equations, motivated by the observation that many symbolic regression benchmarks admit compact signomial structure. ECSEL directly constructs a structural, closed-form expression that serves as both a classifier and an explanation. On standard symbolic regression benchmarks, our method recovers a larger fraction of target equations than competing state-of-the-art approaches while requiring substantially less computation. Leveraging this efficiency, ECSEL achieves classification accuracy competitive with established machine learning models without sacrificing interpretability. Further, we show that ECSEL satisfies some desirable properties regarding global feature behavior, decision-boundary analysis, and local feature attributions. Experiments on benchmark datasets and two real-world case studies i.e., e-commerce and fraud detection, demonstrate that the learned equations expose dataset biases, support counterfactual reasoning, and yield actionable insights.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ECSEL方法，通过学习符号方程实现可解释分类，兼具高精度与可解释性。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有机器学习模型如深度神经网络可解释性差，限制其在敏感领域的应用。符号回归方法虽然能生成可解释表达式，但计算成本高且恢复率低。而许多符号回归基准问题本身具有紧凑的符号结构，若直接学习符号方程形式，可能同时实现高效训练与可解释性。因此，ECSEL旨在获得一个结构化的闭式表达式，既作为分类器又作为解释。
light_analysis.method: ECSEL直接构造形如符号方程的结构化闭式表达式作为分类器。该方法基于符号回归基准中常见紧凑符号结构的观察，通过高效优化学习符号方程参数。与现有符号回归方法相比，ECSEL计算量显著降低。训练过程中，模型自动构建包含加减乘除及幂运算的组合，输出一个可直接读懂的数学公式作为分类决策规则。
light_analysis.result: 在标准符号回归基准上，ECSEL恢复的目标方程比例高于当前最优方法，且计算量大幅减少。在分类任务上，ECSEL达到与XGBoost、随机森林等传统模型相当的精度，同时完全可解释。实验还表明，学习到的方程能够暴露数据集中的隐性偏见，支持反事实推理（如预测改变某个特征值会如何改变分类），并在电商欺诈检测等真实场景中提供可操作的见解。
light_analysis.conclusion: ECSEL通过符号方程学习实现了可解释分类，在保持竞争力的分类性能的同时，提供了全局特征行为分析、决策边界分析和局部特征归因等理想性质。其实际应用价值体现在能揭示数据偏见并支持反事实推理，为高风险场景提供可验证、可信任的决策依据。未来可扩展至回归任务或融合更复杂的符号结构。

title: Chain Of Thought Compression: A Theoretical Analysis
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) has unlocked advanced reasoning abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) with intermediate steps, yet incurs prohibitive computational costs due to generation of extra tokens. Recent studies empirically show that compressing reasoning steps into latent states, or implicit CoT compression, offers a token-efficient alternative. However, the mechanism behind CoT compression remains unclear. In this paper, we provide the first theoretical analysis of the difficulty of learning to internalize intermediate reasoning steps. By introducing Order-r Interaction, we prove that the learning signal for high-order logical dependencies exponentially decays to solve irreducible problem, where skipping intermediate steps inevitably leads to high-order interaction barriers. To empirically validate this, we introduce NatBool-DAG, a challenging benchmark designed to enforce irreducible logical reasoning and eliminate semantic shortcuts. Guided by our theoretical findings, we propose ALiCoT (Aligned Implicit CoT), a novel framework that overcomes the signal decay by aligning latent token distributions with intermediate reasoning states. Experimental results demonstrate that ALiCoT successfully unlocks efficient reasoning: it achieves a 54.4x speedup while maintaining performance comparable to explicit CoT.

light_analysis.overview: 首次从理论上分析隐式思维链压缩的难度，提出Order-r Interaction解释信号衰减，并设计ALiCoT框架实现高效推理。
light_analysis.motivation: 思维链（CoT）虽提升大模型推理能力，但生成中间步骤导致高昂计算成本。现有压缩方法虽减少token，但其内部机制不明。本文旨在从理论角度解释隐式CoT压缩为何困难，并解决信号衰减问题，以实现高效且性能相当的推理。
light_analysis.method: 引入Order-r Interaction理论，证明高阶逻辑依赖的学习信号指数衰减，导致不可约推理问题。为验证理论，构建NatBool-DAG基准，强制无捷径逻辑推理。提出ALiCoT框架，通过对齐潜在token分布与中间推理状态克服信号衰减，实现隐式CoT高效推理。
light_analysis.result: ALiCoT在NatBool-DAG等任务上实现54.4倍加速，同时保持与显式CoT相当的性能。实验验证了理论预测的信号衰减现象，并证明对齐方法有效恢复推理能力。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文首次为隐式CoT压缩提供理论解释，揭示学习高阶依赖的固有困难，并提出ALiCoT解决方案。该工作为高效推理模型设计奠定理论基础，未来可探索更复杂的推理压缩或应用于多模态场景。

title: Dissecting Multimodal In-Context Learning: Modality Asymmetries and Circuit Dynamics in modern Transformers
abstract: Transformer-based multimodal large language models often exhibit in-context learning (ICL) abilities. Motivated by this phenomenon, we ask: how do transformers learn to associate information across modalities from in-context examples? We investigate this question through controlled experiments on small transformers trained on synthetic classification tasks, enabling precise manipulation of data statistics and model architecture. We begin by revisiting core principles of unimodal ICL in modern transformers. While several prior findings replicate, we find that Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) increases the data complexity threshold for ICL. Extending to the multimodal setting reveals a fundamental learning asymmetry: when pretrained on high-diversity data from a primary modality, surprisingly low data complexity in the secondary modality suffices for multimodal ICL to emerge. Mechanistic analysis shows that both settings rely on an induction-style mechanism that copies labels from matching in-context exemplars; multimodal training refines and extends these circuits across modalities. Our findings provide a mechanistic foundation for understanding multimodal ICL in modern transformers and introduce a controlled testbed for future investigation. Code is available at: https://github.com/YiranHuangIrene/multimodal-icl

light_analysis.overview: 通过受控实验研究多模态上下文学习中的模态不对称性和电路动力学，发现RoPE增加数据复杂度阈值，且主模态高多样性可降低辅模态复杂度需求。
light_analysis.motivation: Transformer多模态大语言模型展现出上下文学习能力，但对跨模态关联信息的机制理解不足。现有研究多聚焦单模态，缺乏对多模态ICL中模态间相互作用和电路动力学的深入分析，作者通过合成分类任务的受控实验填补这一空白。
light_analysis.method: 在小型Transformer上使用合成分类任务进行受控实验，精确操纵数据统计和模型架构。首先复现单模态ICL原理，发现RoPE增加数据复杂度阈值；然后引入多模态设置，分析模态不对称性。通过机制分析揭示两种设置均依赖归纳式标签复制机制，多模态训练细化并扩展这些电路。
light_analysis.result: 主模态预训练高多样性数据时，辅模态极低数据复杂度即可实现多模态ICL，揭示学习不对称性。RoPE增加单模态ICL的数据复杂度阈值。多模态训练优化了跨模态的归纳式电路，为理解多模态ICL提供机制基础。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提供了多模态ICL在transformer中的机制基础，包括模态不对称性和电路动力学。引入可控测试平台供未来研究。学术价值在于揭示多模态ICL的机制，应用价值在于指导多模态模型设计和训练数据策略。

title: LLMs versus the Halting Problem: Characterizing Program Termination Reasoning
abstract: Determining whether a program terminates is a central problem in computer science. Turing's Halting Problem established termination as undecidable, showing that no algorithm can universally determine termination for all programs and inputs. Hence, verification tools approximate termination, sometimes failing to prove or disprove; these tools rely on problem specific architectures, and are usually tied to particular programming languages. Recent advances in LLMs raise a natural question: To what extent can they reason about program termination? We evaluate frontier LLMs on a diverse set of C programs from the International Competition on Software Verification (SV Comp) 2025. Our results show that GPT-5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieve scores comparable to top ranked verification tools (with test time scaling). However, while models often correctly infer whether programs terminate, they frequently fail to construct a witness as formal proof, revealing a gap between semantic recognition and symbolic proof generation. Performance further degrades as code length increases. To analyze this gap, we introduce a divergence precondition formulation that characterizes non termination conditions as logical constraints. We hope these findings motivate future research on real-world termination benchmarks, neuro-symbolic approaches that combine LLMs with symbolic verification methods, and, more broadly LLM reasoning on other undecidable problems.

light_analysis.overview: 评估前沿LLM在程序终止推理上的能力，发现其与顶级验证工具得分相当，但语义识别与符号证明存在差距，并提出散度前提公式化非终止条件。
light_analysis.motivation: 程序终止判定是计算机科学的核心问题，但图灵可停问题表明其普遍不可判定。现有验证工具通过近似方法处理终止性问题，但通常依赖特定编程语言和问题特定架构。近期LLM的进步引发了对其在终止推理方面能力的疑问，目前缺乏系统评估。
light_analysis.method: 在SV Comp 2025的多样C程序集上测试GPT-5和Claude Sonnet 4.5等前沿LLM。引入散度前提公式化非终止条件为逻辑约束，分析LLM语义识别（正确推断终止性）与符号证明（构建形式证明）之间的差距，并探究代码长度对性能的影响。
light_analysis.result: LLM（尤其是GPT-5和Claude Sonnet 4.5）在测试时缩放下得分与顶级验证工具相当。然而，模型经常能正确推断程序是否终止，但无法构造作为正式证明的见证，表明语义识别与符号证明生成之间存在鸿沟。性能随代码长度增加而下降。
light_analysis.conclusion: LLM在终止推理上展现出潜力，但符号证明能力不足，需进一步研究。希望推动实际终止基准、神经符号方法结合LLM与符号验证，以及LLM在其他不可判定问题上的推理。

title: AI Agent for Reverse-Engineering Legacy Finite-Difference Code and Translating to Devito
abstract: To facilitate the transformation of legacy finite difference implementations into the Devito environment, this study develops an integrated AI agent framework. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and open-source Large Language Models are combined through multi-stage iterative workflows in the system's hybrid LangGraph architecture. The agent constructs an extensive Devito knowledge graph through document parsing, structure-aware segmentation, extraction of entity relationships, and Leiden-based community detection. GraphRAG optimisation enhances query performance across semantic communities that include seismic wave simulation, computational fluid dynamics, and performance tuning libraries. A reverse engineering component derives three-level query strategies for RAG retrieval through static analysis of Fortran source code. To deliver precise contextual information for language model guidance, the multi-stage retrieval pipeline performs parallel searching, concept expansion, community-scale retrieval, and semantic similarity analysis. Code synthesis is governed by Pydantic-based constraints to guarantee structured outputs and reliability. A comprehensive validation framework integrates conventional static analysis with the G-Eval approach, covering execution correctness, structural soundness, mathematical consistency, and API compliance. The overall agent workflow is implemented on the LangGraph framework and adopts concurrent processing to support quality-based iterative refinement and state-aware dynamic routing. The principal contribution lies in the incorporation of feedback mechanisms motivated by reinforcement learning, enabling a transition from static code translation toward dynamic and adaptive analytical behavior.

light_analysis.overview: 开发集成了RAG和开源大模型的AI Agent框架，用于将遗留有限差分代码逆向工程并翻译为Devito环境。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统有限差分代码向Devito环境转换困难，现有方法缺乏自动化、自适应能力。该研究旨在通过AI Agent实现动态、智能的代码翻译，解决静态翻译的局限性。
light_analysis.method: 采用混合LangGraph架构，结合RAG和开源LLM，构建Devito知识图谱（通过文档解析、实体关系提取、Leiden社区检测），对Fortran源码静态分析生成三层查询策略，多阶段检索管道（并行搜索、概念扩展、社区检索、语义相似度分析），Pydantic约束代码合成，G-Eval验证框架确保正确性，并引入强化学习驱动的反馈机制实现自适应迭代。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确报告具体实验结果数据，但验证框架涵盖执行正确性、结构健全性、数学一致性和API合规性，通过G-Eval和静态分析保证翻译质量。
light_analysis.conclusion: 核心贡献是引入强化学习启发的反馈机制，将静态代码翻译转变为动态自适应分析行为，提升了遗留代码向Devito迁移的自动化水平，具有工程应用价值。

title: RadJEPA: Radiology Encoder for Chest X-Rays via Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture
abstract: Vision-language pretraining has driven much of the recent progress in medical image representation learning, but this paradigm is constrained by the availability of paired image-text data and by the reporting bias of clinical narratives. We ask whether competitive radiology encoders can be learned without any language supervision. We introduce RadJEPA, a self-supervised framework built on a Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture and pretrained on approximately 840K unlabeled chest X-ray images. The model learns to predict latent representations of masked target regions from a visible context region, an objective that differs from both image-text contrastive pretraining and DINO-style self-distillation by explicitly modelling conditional structure in representation space. We evaluate RadJEPA primarily on radiology report generation with a frozen Vicuna-7B decoder, and additionally substitute its encoder into four widely used vision-language backbones (MedLLaVA, Qwen-2.5, BLIP-2, and Phi-4). For completeness we also report disease classification and semantic segmentation results. Across two datasets and four metrics, RadJEPA matches or exceeds the strongest image-only and vision-language baselines while using a ViT-B/14 backbone at 224 x 224 resolution.

light_analysis.overview: 提出RadJEPA，一种无需语言监督的联合嵌入预测架构，用于胸部X光图像的自监督表示学习。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有的医学图像表示学习严重依赖视觉-语言预训练，这受到配对图像-文本数据稀缺和临床叙述报告偏差的限制。作者质疑是否可以在没有任何语言监督的情况下学习有竞争力的放射学编码器，旨在探索纯图像自监督方法的潜力。
light_analysis.method: RadJEPA基于联合嵌入预测架构（JEPA），在约84万张未标记的胸部X光图像上预训练。模型学习从可见上下文区域预测被遮蔽目标区域的潜在表示，与图像-文本对比预训练和DINO式自蒸馏不同，它显式建模表示空间中的条件结构。使用ViT-B/14骨干网络，224×224分辨率。
light_analysis.result: 主要评估放射学报告生成任务，使用冻结的Vicuna-7B解码器，并在四个视觉-语言骨干（MedLLaVA、Qwen-2.5、BLIP-2、Phi-4）中替换编码器。在两个数据集和四个指标上，RadJEPA匹配或超过最强的纯图像和视觉-语言基线。此外，在疾病分类和语义分割上也有报告。
light_analysis.conclusion: RadJEPA证明纯图像自监督学习可以达到与视觉-语言预训练相当甚至更优的性能，减少了对配对数据的依赖，具有重要的学术和实际应用价值。未来可探索更大规模数据和更高分辨率下的表现，以及与其他自监督任务的结合。

title: LuxRemix: Lighting Decomposition and Remixing for Indoor Scenes
abstract: We present a novel approach for interactive light editing in indoor scenes from a single multi-view scene capture. Our method leverages a generative image-based light decomposition model that factorizes complex indoor scene illumination into its constituent light sources. This factorization enables independent manipulation of individual light sources, specifically allowing control over their state (on/off), chromaticity, and intensity. We further introduce multi-view lighting harmonization to ensure consistent propagation of the lighting decomposition across all scene views. This is integrated into a relightable 3D Gaussian splatting representation, providing real-time interactive control over the individual light sources. Our results demonstrate highly photorealistic lighting decomposition and relighting outcomes across diverse indoor scenes. We evaluate our method on both synthetic and real-world datasets and provide a quantitative and qualitative comparison to state-of-the-art techniques. For video results and interactive demos, see https://luxremix.github.io.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种基于生成模型的室内场景光照分解与交互编辑方法，支持独立光源控制并集成到3D高斯泼溅表示中实现实时重光照。
light_analysis.motivation: 室内场景光照复杂，现有方法难以实现交互式独立光源编辑，缺乏对复杂照明进行可分解且实时控制的手段。本方法旨在解决该问题，满足虚拟现实、电影制作等领域对灵活光照编辑的需求。
light_analysis.method: 采用生成式图像光照分解模型，将室内场景多视角捕获的复杂照明分解为独立光源，支持控制其开关、色度和强度。引入多视角照明一致性，确保分解跨视图和谐。集成到可重光照的3D高斯泼溅表示中，实现实时交互编辑。
light_analysis.result: 在合成和真实数据集上取得高度逼真的光照分解与重光照效果，与现有技术进行定量定性比较，性能优越。提供视频结果和交互演示，验证方法的实用性和实时性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该方法实现了室内场景交互式光照编辑，具有高度逼真和实时性，为光照控制提供新范式。未来可扩展至更复杂场景或结合物理模拟，但依赖多视角输入可能为实际应用带来限制。

title: Drive-P2D: A Progressive Perception-to-Decision Benchmark for VLMs in Autonomous Driving
abstract: Autonomous driving requires reliable perception and safe decision-making in complex scenarios. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) demonstrate reasoning and generalization abilities, opening new possibilities for autonomous driving; however, existing benchmarks often evaluate perception and decision-making separately, limit failure analysis with choice-only formats, or introduce evaluation bias through LLM-scored long-form outputs. To address these issues, we present Drive-P2D, a progressive perception-to-decision benchmark with 6,650 questions across Object, Scene, and Decision levels. Drive-P2D adopts a separated reasoning-and-answer protocol: final answers are scored objectively, while reasoning is analyzed to identify error modes exposed along the progressive perception-to-decision chain. We evaluate mainstream VLMs across all and high-risk scenarios, and further characterize the perception-to-decision capability boundary through correlation analysis and similar-scene robustness testing. Reasoning further exposes failure modes such as logical reasoning errors and semantic feature omissions, and we train a lightweight analyzer model to automate large-scale error-mode annotation of reasoning. Together, these designs provide practical insights for building safer and more reliable VLMs for real-world autonomous driving.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Drive-P2D基准，渐进式感知到决策的评估，含6650个问题，支持客观评分和推理错误分析，用于评估VLM在自动驾驶中的能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有自动驾驶基准将感知和决策分开评估，限制故障分析仅用选择格式，或通过LLM评分的长文本输出引入评估偏差。这些问题阻碍了全面评估视觉语言模型（VLM）在真实场景中的感知到决策能力，尤其是错误模式的识别。
light_analysis.method: 构建Drive-P2D基准，包含Object、Scene、Decision三个渐进层次的6650个问题。采用分离的推理-答案协议：答案客观评分，推理用于识别错误模式。评估主流VLM在所有场景和高风险场景，通过相关性分析和相似场景鲁棒性测试刻画能力边界。训练轻量级分析器自动化大规模推理错误标注。
light_analysis.result: 实验揭示VLM在感知到决策链上的错误模式，如逻辑推理错误和语义特征遗漏。通过客观评分和推理分析，提供了对VLM能力边界的定量刻画。摘要未明确给出具体性能数据，但指出方法能够有效识别失败模式。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出了更全面的VLM评估框架，促进自动驾驶安全性与可靠性。通过客观评分和自动化错误标注，为模型改进提供实用见解。未来可扩展至更多场景和模型，但摘要未提及局限性。

title: Left-Right Symmetry Breaking in CLIP-style Vision-Language Models Trained on Synthetic Spatial-Relation Data
abstract: Spatial understanding remains a key challenge in vision-language models. Yet it is still unclear whether such understanding is truly acquired, and if so, through what mechanisms. We present a controllable 1D image-text testbed to probe how left-right relational understanding emerges in Transformer-based vision and text encoders trained with a CLIP-style contrastive objective. We train lightweight Transformer-based vision and text encoders end-to-end on paired descriptions of one- and two-object scenes and evaluate generalization to unseen object pairs while systematically varying label and layout diversity. We find that contrastive training learns left-right relations and that label diversity, more than layout diversity, is the primary driver of generalization in this setting. To gain the mechanistic understanding, we perform an attention decomposition and show that interactions between positional and token embeddings induce a horizontal attention gradient that breaks left-right symmetry in the encoders; ablating this contribution substantially reduces left-right discrimination. Our results provide a mechanistic insight of when and how CLIP-style models acquire relational competence.

light_analysis.overview: 揭示CLIP风格模型中左右关系理解的机制，发现标签多样性和注意力梯度导致对称性破坏。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有视觉语言模型在空间理解上仍存在挑战，尤其是左右关系。尽管模型在任务上表现良好，但其是否真正获得关系理解以及通过何种机制尚不明确。这阻碍了模型的可靠性和泛化能力提升，因此需要可解释的机制分析。
light_analysis.method: 构建可控1D图像文本测试床，使用轻量级Transformer编码器，以CLIP对比目标端到端训练。系统改变标签和布局多样性，评估对未见对象对的泛化。通过注意力分解，分析位置与标记嵌入的交互作用，量化水平注意力梯度对左右对称性的影响。
light_analysis.result: 对比训练能有效学习左右关系，标签多样性是泛化的主要驱动因素，而非布局多样性。注意力分解显示，位置与标记嵌入的交互产生水平注意力梯度，破坏编码器的左右对称性；去除该贡献后左右判别能力显著下降。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究提供了CLIP模型获取关系能力的机制性见解：左右关系学习依赖于标签多样性，且注意力中的对称性破坏是关键。这有助于设计更好的空间理解模型，但本研究限于合成1D数据，未来需验证在真实3D场景中的适用性。

title: Representation-Aware Unlearning via Activation Signatures: From Suppression to Entity-Signature Erasure
abstract: Entity-level unlearning is usually evaluated by what a model says: whether it stops naming the target, refuses a query, or shifts a Truth Ratio distribution. These output-level tests, however, do not show whether a subject's internal representation has been attenuated. We introduce the Entity Representation Unlearning Framework (ERUF), a representation-aware framework that mines subject-specific activation signatures, suppresses the corresponding activation direction, and distills the behavior into LoRA parameters. Among evaluated baselines, ERUF is the only method that jointly achieves surface-level suppression, internal attenuation, and utility preservation. On TOFU forget10, ERUF achieves FQ = 0.99 and MU = 0.62, matching reported oracle utility while approaching oracle forget quality. Across most standard foundation-model settings, ERUF maintains low leakage and low internal target activation, with SMR between 0.00% and 1.10%, EL10 below 0.06, and utility drift below 3%. On Llama-3.1-8B, adversarial entity recovery falls from 63.89% to 20.15%, while name-agnostic recovery decreases by 72.7% to 77.4%. Joint surface/internal diagnostics further reveal scale-dependent behavior in reasoning-prior models that surface metrics alone would miss. We interpret these results as operational evidence of representation-level attenuation, not as a formal guarantee of irreversible deletion.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ERUF框架，通过激活签名挖掘和压制实现表示层面的实体遗忘，兼顾表面抑制、内部衰减和效用保持。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有实体级遗忘评估主要依赖输出层面测试（如命名目标、拒绝查询、Truth Ratio分布偏移），但这些测试无法证明模型内部表示已被削弱。内部表示衰减对于确保真正的遗忘至关重要，而现有方法通常只关注表面行为，忽略了表示层面的影响。因此，需要一种表示感知的遗忘框架来同时实现表面抑制和内部衰减，并保持模型效用。
light_analysis.method: ERUF框架首先通过前向传播挖掘主题特定的激活签名，即模型内部对实体敏感的神经元激活模式。然后，通过梯度更新压制这些激活方向，抑制对应表示。最后，将压制的行为通过知识蒸馏迁移到LoRA参数中，实现高效参数更新。该方法无需修改模型架构，仅需微调少量LoRA参数，同时联合优化表面抑制、内部衰减和效用保持三个目标。
light_analysis.result: 在TOFU forget10数据集上，ERUF达到FQ=0.99、MU=0.62，匹配oracle的遗忘质量和效用。在标准基础模型设置中，SMR在0.00%-1.10%之间，EL10低于0.06，效用漂移低于3%。在Llama-3.1-8B上，对抗性实体恢复率从63.89%降至20.15%，名称无关恢复降低72.7%-77.4%。联合表面/内部诊断揭示了推理优先模型中仅靠表面指标无法发现的规模依赖行为。
light_analysis.conclusion: ERUF通过操作激活签名实现了表示层面的实体遗忘，提供了表面抑制和内部衰减的证据，但不可逆删除无法保证。该方法在多个基础模型上表现良好，尤其适用于需要兼顾遗忘质量与模型效用的场景。未来工作可探索更严格的表示删除保证，并扩展到更复杂的实体类型。

title: Clozing the Gap: Exploring Why Language Model Surprisal Outperforms Cloze Surprisal
abstract: How predictable a word is can be quantified in two ways: using human responses to the cloze task or using probabilities from language models (LMs).When used as predictors of processing effort, LM probabilities outperform probabilities derived from cloze data. However, it is important to establish that LM probabilities do so for the right reasons, since different predictors can lead to different scientific conclusions about the role of prediction in language comprehension. We present evidence for three hypotheses about the advantage of LM probabilities: not suffering from low resolution, distinguishing semantically similar words, and accurately assigning probabilities to low-frequency words. These results call for efforts to improve the resolution of cloze studies, coupled with experiments on whether human-like prediction is also as sensitive to the fine-grained distinctions made by LM probabilities.
comment: 18 pages, 10 figures, accepted to ACL 2026 Main Conference

light_analysis.overview: 探究语言模型概率优于完型填空概率的原因，提出并验证三个假设，呼吁改进完型填空研究分辨率。
light_analysis.motivation: 语言模型概率在预测处理难度上优于完型填空概率，但需确认其优势源于正确原因，否则可能导致关于预测在语言理解中作用的错误科学结论。现有完型填空概率存在分辨率低、无法区分语义相似词、低频词概率不准确等不足，亟待解释LM概率的优势来源。
light_analysis.method: 提出三个假设解释LM概率的优势：不遭受低分辨率问题、能区分语义相似词、准确分配低频词概率。通过收集LM概率与完型填空概率的证据进行验证，但摘要未明确说明具体模型、数据集或实验设计。
light_analysis.result: 证据支持三个假设：LM概率避免了低分辨率问题，能区分语义相似词，对低频词分配更准确的概率。然而，摘要未提供量化性能指标或与基线方法的详细对比，仅定性描述LM概率的优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文贡献在于系统分析LM概率优于完型填空概率的原因，推动对预测在语言理解中作用的理解。呼吁改进完型填空研究的分辨率，并建议未来实验探究人类预测是否如LM概率一样对细粒度区分敏感。局限性在于未提供具体数据支撑，需后续实证研究。

title: Beyond Transfer Accuracy: Faithful Circuits for Controlled Low-Resource Adaptation
abstract: Existing circuit discovery methods rely on templated tasks with clean counterfactuals, limiting their use on diverse natural text. We adapt Contextual Decomposition for Transformers (CD-T) for unstructured settings via label-balanced activation means and task-directional relevance scoring, enabling counterfactual-free circuit discovery. We leverage these circuits for Circuit-Targeted Supervised Fine-Tuning (CT-SFT), restricting parameter updates to task-relevant heads and LayerNorm. Experiments on NusaX cross-lingual sentiment transfer show that CT-SFT is highly competitive for low-resource adaptation. While non-circuit sparse updates and full fine-tuning sometimes match target accuracy through capacity recruitment, CT-SFT uniquely minimizes catastrophic forgetting, preserving source-language and related-task performance. Extensions to XNLI confirm these findings hold across broader tasks and model families, demonstrating that circuit-targeted adaptation provides a safer, causally grounded alternative to global fine-tuning.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种无需反事实的电路发现方法，并利用电路靶向微调实现低资源适应，最小化灾难性遗忘。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有电路发现方法依赖模板化任务和清晰的反事实样本，限制了在多样化自然文本中的应用。低资源适应中，全微调容易导致灾难性遗忘，破坏源语言和相关任务性能。因此需要一种更安全的、因果驱动的微调方法。
light_analysis.method: 基于Contextual Decomposition for Transformers (CD-T)，引入标签平衡激活均值和任务方向相关性评分，实现无反事实的电路发现。然后提出Circuit-Targeted Supervised Fine-Tuning (CT-SFT)，仅更新任务相关的注意力头和LayerNorm参数。在NusaX跨语言情感数据集和XNLI上评估。
light_analysis.result: 在NusaX跨语言情感迁移任务中，CT-SFT在低资源适应上具有竞争力。虽然非电路稀疏更新和全微调有时能达到相似的目标准确率，但CT-SFT显著减少灾难性遗忘，保持源语言和相关任务性能。扩展到XNLI也证实了有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出一种因果驱动的电路靶向微调方法，优于全局微调，提供更安全的低资源适应策略。未来可探索更广泛的电路应用。

title: Seeing vs. Believing: Evaluating the Language Bias of Open-Source MLLMs in Counter-Intuitive Scenes
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance in mainstream visual understanding tasks, but their ability to process action scenes that contradict everyday common sense remains undertested. To address this gap, we introduce CAIT, a benchmark comprising 400 high-fidelity synthetic scenes focused on counter-intuitive visual actions, such as ``a rabbit is chasing a tiger'', where visual evidence explicitly contradicts common-sense expectations. We evaluate human, leading proprietary models (e.g., Claude and Gemini), and 14 representative open-source MLLMs. Humans achieve near-perfect performance (around 0.95 accuracy) and proprietary models demonstrate robust understanding (achieving up to 0.88 accuracy), standard open-source instruction-tuned models perform at the chance level. Further analysis demonstrates that this failure is driven by a strong language prior: rather than trusting the visual input, they automatically override the anomalous visual signals with statistically common text descriptions. Although introducing Chain-of-Thought reasoning mechanisms can improve accuracy, it significantly slows down the response and generates a new failure mode: models overthink the scenario and refuse to accept the actual visual content simply because it violates real-world physical laws. Finally, we demonstrate that targeted fine-tuning and structured prompting can effectively mitigate this reliance on language priors, enabling open-source models to accurately ground their reasoning in actual visual evidence.

light_analysis.overview: 提出CAIT基准测试，揭示开源多模态大模型在处理反直觉视觉场景时存在的语言先验偏见，并提出缓解方法。
light_analysis.motivation: 当前多模态大模型在主流视觉任务中表现优异，但处理违反常识的动作场景（如“兔子追老虎”）的能力尚未充分测试。现有方法过度依赖统计共现的语言先验，忽视视觉证据。该研究通过构建CAIT基准，系统评估开源模型的反直觉场景理解，发现其准确率近乎随机，根源在于语言偏见而非视觉理解。
light_analysis.method: 构建包含400个高保真合成场景的CAIT基准，每个场景呈现反直觉的视觉动作（如兔子追老虎）。评估人类、专有模型（Claude、Gemini）和14个开源MLLM。通过对比分析揭示语言先验的影响，并实验链式思维推理、微调及结构化提示等方法，以增强模型对视觉证据的依赖。
light_analysis.result: 人类准确率约0.95，专有模型最高0.88，开源指令微调模型表现接近随机（约0.5）。链式思维推理可提升准确率但显著降低响应速度，并产生“过度思考”问题（拒绝接受违反物理规律的视觉内容）。目标微调和结构化提示可有效缓解语言偏见，使开源模型正确基于视觉证据推理。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究揭示了开源MLLMs在反直觉场景中的语言偏见缺陷，并提供了CAIT基准作为评估工具。通过微调和提示工程可提升模型对视觉证据的信任度，但需平衡速度与正确性。未来可探索更高效的偏见消除方法，推动多模态模型在现实复杂场景中的可靠应用。

title: TowerMind: A Tower Defence Game Learning Environment and Benchmark for LLM as Agents
abstract: Recent breakthroughs in Large Language Models (LLMs) have positioned them as a promising paradigm for agents, with long-term planning and decision-making emerging as core general-purpose capabilities for adapting to diverse scenarios and tasks. Real-time strategy (RTS) games serve as an ideal testbed for evaluating these two capabilities, as their inherent gameplay requires both macro-level strategic planning and micro-level tactical adaptation and action execution. Existing RTS game-based environments either suffer from relatively high computational demands or lack support for textual observations, which has constrained the use of RTS games for LLM evaluation. Motivated by this, we present TowerMind, a novel environment grounded in the tower defense (TD) subgenre of RTS games. TowerMind preserves the key evaluation strengths of RTS games for assessing LLMs, while featuring low computational demands and a multimodal observation space, including pixel-based, textual, and structured game-state representations. In addition, TowerMind supports the evaluation of model hallucination and provides a high degree of customizability. We design five benchmark levels to evaluate several widely used LLMs under different multimodal input settings. The results reveal a clear performance gap between LLMs and human experts across both capability and hallucination dimensions. The experiments further highlight key limitations in LLM behavior, such as inadequate planning validation, a lack of multifinality in decision-making, and inefficient action use. We also evaluate two classic reinforcement learning algorithms: Ape-X DQN and PPO. By offering a lightweight and multimodal design, TowerMind complements the existing RTS game-based environment landscape and introduces a new benchmark for the AI agent field. The source code is publicly available on GitHub(https://github.com/tb6147877/TowerMind).

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于塔防游戏的轻量级多模态环境TowerMind，用于评估LLM代理的长期规划与决策能力。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM作为代理需要长期规划和决策能力，实时策略游戏是理想测试床，但现有环境计算需求高或缺乏文本观测，限制了LLM评估。TowerMind通过低计算需求、多模态观测空间和幻觉评估，填补了这一空白。
light_analysis.method: TowerMind基于塔防游戏，支持像素、文本和结构化游戏状态多模态观测，具有低计算需求和高度可定制性。设计五个基准级别，评估多种LLM在不同多模态输入设置下的表现，并与Ape-X DQN和PPO等经典强化学习算法对比。
light_analysis.result: LLM与人类专家在能力和幻觉维度存在显著差距，表现包括规划验证不足、决策缺乏多样性、行动效率低。评估显示LLM性能低于人类，而经典RL算法也有类似局限。
light_analysis.conclusion: TowerMind为LLM代理评估提供轻量级、多模态环境，补充了现有RTS游戏基准，强调其在实际应用中的潜力。代码已开源，未来可用于更广泛的AI代理研究。

title: TAGRPO: Boosting GRPO on Image-to-Video Generation with Direct Trajectory Alignment
abstract: Recent studies have demonstrated the efficacy of integrating Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) into flow matching models, particularly for text-to-image and text-to-video generation. However, we find that directly applying these techniques to image-to-video (I2V) models often fails to yield consistent reward improvements. To address this limitation, we present TAGRPO, a robust post-training framework for I2V models inspired by contrastive learning. Our approach is grounded in the observation that rollout videos generated from identical initial noise provide superior guidance for optimization. Leveraging this insight, we propose a novel GRPO loss applied to intermediate latents, encouraging direct alignment with high-reward trajectories while maximizing distance from low-reward counterparts. Furthermore, we introduce a memory bank for rollout videos to enhance diversity and reduce computational overhead. Despite its simplicity, TAGRPO achieves significant improvements over DanceGRPO in I2V generation. The deliverables will be updated at https://tagrpo.github.io/ .

light_analysis.overview: 提出TAGRPO框架，通过对比学习对齐高奖励轨迹，解决GRPO在图像到视频生成中的失效问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有GRPO方法在文本到图像和文本到视频生成中有效，但直接应用于图像到视频（I2V）模型时难以稳定提升奖励。I2V任务对初始条件和轨迹一致性要求更高，现有方法未充分利用生成轨迹中的对比信息。因此需要一种新的后训练框架，能够利用相同初始噪声的生成视频作为引导，实现更高效的奖励优化。
light_analysis.method: TAGRPO基于对比学习，核心创新包括：1）利用相同初始噪声生成的rollout视频提供优化指导；2）设计针对中间潜变量的GRPO损失，直接对齐高奖励轨迹同时远离低奖励轨迹；3）引入记忆库存储rollout视频，增强多样性并降低计算开销。整体框架简单高效，适用于现有I2V模型的后训练。
light_analysis.result: 在图像到视频生成任务上，TAGRPO相比DanceGRPO取得显著提升。摘要未提供具体数值指标，但声称“significant improvements”。实验结果验证了所提方法在奖励优化和生成质量上的有效性，记忆库设计有效平衡了多样性与计算效率。
light_analysis.conclusion: TAGRPO为I2V模型提供了一种有效的后训练框架，通过对比式轨迹对齐解决了GRPO泛化问题。其价值在于将强化学习与对比学习结合，拓展了视频生成模型的优化手段。未来可探索更复杂的奖励函数或应用于其他生成任务。局限性可能在于对初始噪声的依赖及记忆库管理开销。

title: Approximate Equivariance via Projection-based Regularisation
abstract: Equivariance is a powerful inductive bias in neural networks, improving generalisation and physical consistency. Recently, however, non-equivariant models have regained attention, due to their better runtime performance and imperfect symmetries that might arise in real-world applications. This has motivated the development of approximately equivariant models that strike a middle ground between respecting symmetries and fitting the data distribution. Existing approaches in this field usually apply sample-based regularisers which depend on data augmentation at training time, incurring a high sample complexity, in particular for continuous groups such as $SO(3)$. This work instead approaches approximate equivariance via a projection-based regulariser which leverages the orthogonal decomposition of linear layers into equivariant and non-equivariant components. In contrast to existing methods, this penalises non-equivariance at an operator level across the full group orbit, rather than point-wise. We present a mathematical framework for computing the non-equivariance penalty exactly and efficiently in both the spatial and spectral domain. In our experiments, our method consistently outperforms prior approximate equivariance approaches in both model performance and efficiency, achieving substantial runtime gains over sample-based regularisers.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于投影的正则化方法实现近似等变性，通过对线性层的等变/非等变分量正交分解，在算子级别进行惩罚，无需数据增强。
light_analysis.motivation: 等变性是神经网络的重要归纳偏置，但实际应用中存在不完全对称性和性能需求，促使发展近似等变模型。现有方法通常采用基于样本的正则化，依赖训练时数据增强，样本复杂度高，尤其对于连续群如SO(3)，计算效率低。因此需要一种更高效、无需数据增强的近似等变方法。
light_analysis.method: 利用线性层在等变与非等变分量上的正交分解，提出投影正则化方法。该方法在算子级别（而非逐点）惩罚非等变性，覆盖整个群轨道。通过数学框架在空间域和谱域精确高效计算惩罚项，无需数据增强，适用于连续群如SO(3)。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，该方法在模型性能和效率上均优于现有近似等变方法（如基于样本的正则化）。具体地，在多个任务上取得更好或相当的准确率，同时运行时显著加快，实现大幅时间增益。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出一种新的近似等变正则化方法，通过投影分解实现高效、无需数据增强的等变约束，兼顾对称性与数据拟合。该方法在理论上可精确计算，实验中性能优越，为处理不完全对称性提供了实用工具。未来可推广至更一般的对称结构。

title: Beyond Binary: Turning Partial Success into Dense Verifiable Rewards for Reinforcement Learning in Code Generation
abstract: Effective reward design is a central challenge in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for code generation. Mainstream test-suite-level outcome rewards enforce functional correctness but induce sparsity, while external Reward Models (RMs) provide dense supervision at the cost of misalignment and additional overhead. Since code evaluation naturally yields multiple test-case-level outcomes, partial success, i.e., passing a subset of test cases, offers an intrinsic, verifiable source of dense supervision. In this paper, we propose VeRPO (Verifiable Dense Reward Policy Optimization), an RL framework that systematically turns verifiable partial success into reliable dense rewards. We analyze partial-success rewards using a weighted sum formulation, theoretically identifying a critical cardinality bias that causes policy updates to disproportionately favor gains from easy-test successes over progress on frontier tests. Based on this, VeRPO introduces a dynamic, density-calibrated local reward that explicitly corrects this bias and provides robust dense supervision from partial success. To enhance alignment with end-to-end functional correctness, VeRPO further integrates the local dense reward with global execution outcomes. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks and settings demonstrate that VeRPO outperforms outcome-driven and RM-based baselines, achieving up to +8.83 pass@1 gain with negligible time cost (< 0.02%) and zero GPU memory overhead.

light_analysis.overview: 提出VeRPO框架，利用代码生成中的部分成功（通过部分测试用例）作为可验证的密集奖励，并纠正基数偏差，显著提升强化学习性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 强化学习在代码生成中面临奖励设计困境：测试集级结果奖励（二元通过/失败）过于稀疏，难以有效引导学习；而外部奖励模型虽提供密集监督，但存在对齐问题、额外计算成本和GPU内存开销。代码评估自然产生多测试用例结果，部分成功（通过部分用例）是内在可验证的密集监督源，但现有方法未充分利用。因此，需要一种既密集可靠又无需额外模型的奖励机制。
light_analysis.method: VeRPO框架基于部分成功设计奖励。首先分析加权和形式的部分成功奖励，发现其存在基数偏差：策略更新偏向于在简单测试用例上获得成功，而忽视在困难用例上的进展。为此，提出动态密度校准局部奖励，显式纠正该偏差，提供稳健的密集监督。此外，将局部密集奖励与全局执行结果（完整通过/失败）结合，增强与最终功能正确性的一致性。实验在多个代码生成基准上进行。
light_analysis.result: 在多种基准和设置下，VeRPO全面优于结果驱动型奖励和基于外部奖励模型的基线方法。在pass@1指标上最高提升+8.83%，而额外时间成本低于0.02%，GPU内存开销为零。这表明VeRPO在提供有效密集奖励的同时，几乎不增加计算负担，具有高效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: VeRPO成功将代码生成中的部分成功转化为可靠密集奖励，解决了稀疏奖励和外部模型开销问题，显著提升了强化学习的代码生成性能。其理论分析（基数偏差）和实用设计（动态校准奖励）具有学术价值。实际应用中，VeRPO可高效集成到现有代码生成流程，未来可探索更细粒度的奖励分解或应用于其他结构化任务。

title: EpiQAL: Benchmarking Large Language Models in Epidemiological Question Answering and Reasoning
abstract: Reliable epidemiological reasoning requires synthesizing study evidence to infer disease burden, transmission dynamics, and intervention effects at the population level. Existing medical question answering benchmarks primarily emphasize clinical knowledge or patient-level reasoning, yet few systematically evaluate evidence-grounded epidemiological inference. We present EpiQAL, the first diagnostic benchmark for epidemiological question answering across diverse diseases, comprising three subsets built from open-access literature. The three subsets progressively test factual recall, multi-step inference, and conclusion reconstruction under incomplete information, and are constructed through a quality-controlled pipeline combining taxonomy guidance, multi-model verification, and difficulty screening. Experiments on fifteen models spanning open-source and proprietary systems reveal that current LLMs show limited performance on epidemiological reasoning, with multi-step inference posing the greatest challenge. Model rankings shift across subsets, and scale alone does not predict success. Chain-of-Thought prompting benefits multi-step inference but yields mixed results elsewhere. EpiQAL provides fine-grained diagnostic signals for evidence-grounding, inferential reasoning, and conclusion reconstruction.

light_analysis.overview: 提出EpiQAL，首个流行病学问答诊断基准，系统性评估大语言模型的证据基础推理能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有医学问答基准主要强调临床知识或患者级推理，缺乏对流行病学推理的系统评估。流行病学推理需要综合研究证据推断疾病负担、传播动态和干预效果，对公共卫生决策至关重要，但当前大语言模型在此领域能力未知。
light_analysis.method: EpiQAL基于开源文献构建三个子集，分别测试事实回忆、多步推理和不完整信息下的结论重建。通过结合分类法指导、多模型验证和难度筛选的质量控制流程，确保基准的诊断价值。实验涵盖15个开源和闭源模型。
light_analysis.result: 大语言模型在流行病学推理上表现有限，多步推理挑战最大。模型排名随子集变化，规模不预测成功。Chain-of-Thought提示对多步推理有益，但在其他子集效果混合。EpiQAL提供了细粒度的诊断信号。
light_analysis.conclusion: EpiQAL是首个系统性评估大语言模型流行病学推理能力的基准，揭示了当前模型的局限，并为改进证据基础、推理推理和结论重建提供了诊断工具。未来可扩展至更多疾病和推理类型。

title: Faithfulness Evaluation for Decoder-only LLM Attributions with Controlled Retained Information
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly evaluated with input attribution methods, yet comparing such explanations remains challenging. Existing soft-perturbation faithfulness metrics, such as Soft-NC and Soft-NS, can conflate attribution quality with the number of words retained during perturbation: attribution methods with larger average scores may keep more words and therefore obtain inflated scores. To address this issue, we propose $π$-Soft-NC and $π$-Soft-NS, an evaluation framework that compares attribution methods under the same expected retaining probability, thus controlling the number of retained words. We further introduce Grad-ELLM, a gradient-based attribution method tailored to autoregressive decoder-only LLMs, which combines gradient-derived channel importance with attention-derived token importance at each decoding step. Experiments on classification and open-generation tasks with Llama and Mistral show that Grad-ELLM achieves strong comprehensiveness-oriented faithfulness under $π$-Soft-NC, while there is no dominant method under $π$-Soft-NS. Our evaluation metric serves as a rigorous framework to compare XAI methods for LLMs, which will support progress in the field.

light_analysis.overview: 提出π-Soft-NC和π-Soft-NS框架，通过控制保留词数公平评估LLM归因方法，并设计Grad-ELLM归因方法。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有软扰动忠实度指标（如Soft-NC、Soft-NS）在比较归因方法时，因保留词数不同导致分数膨胀，无法公平对比。自回归解码器LLM缺乏专门的梯度归因方法，需要更严格的评估框架来准确衡量归因质量。
light_analysis.method: 提出π-Soft-NC和π-Soft-NS，通过固定期望保留概率控制保留词数，实现公平比较。设计Grad-ELLM，结合每步解码的梯度通道重要性与注意力词元重要性，专用于自回归解码器LLM。
light_analysis.result: 在分类和开放生成任务上使用Llama和Mistral模型，Grad-ELLM在π-Soft-NC下取得强完整性忠实度，但π-Soft-NS下无主导方法，表明不同指标下性能差异。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该框架为LLM可解释性方法比较提供严格基准，有助于领域发展。Grad-ELLM在特定场景有效，但需进一步优化以应对不同忠实度度量。

title: EHRSummarizer: A Privacy-Aware, FHIR-Native Reference Architecture for Source-Grounded EHR Summarization
abstract: Clinicians routinely navigate fragmented electronic health record (EHR) interfaces to assemble a coherent picture of a patient's problems, medications, recent encounters, and longitudinal trends. This manuscript describes EHRSummarizer, a privacy-aware, FHIR-native reference architecture for structured EHR summarization. The architecture retrieves a targeted set of high-yield HL7 FHIR R4 resources, normalizes them into a clinical context package, and uses a constrained summarization stage to produce source-grounded summaries intended to support chart review. The architecture further clarifies missing-data status handling, medication-status ambiguity, controlled use of narrative clinical documents when available, and future source-to-summary traceability. The manuscript describes a reference architecture and prototype behavior rather than a validated clinical intervention, autonomous clinical decision-support system, or evidence of clinical benefit. Prototype demonstrations on synthetic and test FHIR environments illustrate end-to-end behavior and output formats; however, this manuscript does not report clinical outcomes, controlled workflow studies, or benchmark results. We outline an evaluation plan centered on faithfulness, omission risk, temporal correctness, usability, privacy, and operational monitoring to guide future institutional assessment.
comment: 15 pages, 2 figures, 2 tables. Version 2 clarifies missing-data status handling, medication-status ambiguity, controlled narrative-document handling, source-grounded resource grouping, and future source-to-summary traceability

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种隐私感知、FHIR原生的参考架构EHRSummarizer，用于生成基于源的结构化EHR摘要，以支持临床图表审查。
light_analysis.motivation: 临床医生经常在碎片化的电子健康记录（EHR）界面中费力地整合患者问题、用药、近期就诊和纵向趋势，现有方法缺乏结构化和源追踪，且隐私保护不足。该架构旨在解决信息碎片化和数据歧义问题，提高摘要的可信度和可用性。
light_analysis.method: 架构通过检索高价值HL7 FHIR R4资源，将其归一化为临床上下文包，并采用受限摘要阶段生成源引用的摘要。关键创新包括处理缺失数据状态、用药状态歧义、控制性使用叙事文档以及未来实现源到摘要的可追溯性。在合成和测试FHIR环境中演示了原型行为。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未报告具体的临床结果或基准测试，仅描述了原型在合成和测试FHIR环境中的端到端行为及输出格式。作者指出这不是验证过的临床干预，也未报告临床收益或对照研究。
light_analysis.conclusion: EHRSummarizer提供了一个隐私感知、FHIR原生的参考架构，为结构化EHR摘奠定了基础，强调源引用和隐私。未来的评估计划围绕忠实度、遗漏风险、时间正确性、可用性、隐私和操作监控展开，尚需进一步临床验证。

title: Guiding Token-Sparse Diffusion Models
abstract: Diffusion models deliver high quality in image synthesis but remain expensive during training and inference. Recent works have leveraged the inherent redundancy in visual content to make training more affordable by training only on a subset of visual information. While these methods were successful in providing cheaper and more effective training, sparsely trained diffusion models struggle in inference. This is due to their lacking response to Classifier-free Guidance (CFG) leading to underwhelming performance during inference. To overcome this, we propose Sparse Guidance (SG). Instead of using conditional dropout as a signal to guide diffusion models, SG uses token-level sparsity. As a result, SG preserves the high-variance of the conditional prediction better, achieving good quality and high variance outputs. Leveraging token-level sparsity at inference, SG improves fidelity at lower compute, achieving 1.58 FID on the commonly used ImageNet-256 benchmark with 25% fewer FLOPs, and yields up to 58% FLOP savings at matched baseline quality. To demonstrate the effectiveness of Sparse Guidance, we train a 2.5B text-to-image diffusion model using training time sparsity and leverage SG during inference. SG achieves improvements in composition and human preference score while increasing throughput at the same time.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Sparse Guidance (SG)方法，利用令牌级稀疏性指导扩散模型，解决稀疏训练模型对无分类器引导响应不足的问题，提升推理质量和效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 扩散模型训练和推理成本高昂，现有方法通过稀疏训练降低训练开销，但稀疏训练模型在推理时对无分类器引导（CFG）响应不足，导致生成质量下降。需要一种在推理时有效利用稀疏性的引导方法，以兼顾质量和计算效率。
light_analysis.method: 提出Sparse Guidance (SG)，替代传统条件丢弃，在推理时使用令牌级稀疏性作为引导信号。SG通过保留条件预测的高方差，提升输出多样性和保真度。结合训练时稀疏性，SG可在减少计算量的同时保持或改善图像质量。论文在ImageNet-256和2.5B文本到图像模型上验证了方法。
light_analysis.result: 在ImageNet-256基准上，SG以1.58的FID超越基线，并节省25%的FLOPs；在匹配基线FID时，FLOPs节省高达58%。在2.5B文本到图像模型上，SG提升了组成质量和人类偏好分数，同时提高了推理吞吐量。
light_analysis.conclusion: Sparse Guidance有效解决了稀疏训练扩散模型的推理局限，在降低计算成本的同时提升生成质量和效率。该方法具有显著的学术价值和实际应用潜力，可推广至大规模扩散模型。未来可探索更优的令牌稀疏策略及与其他引导方法的结合。

title: InfoSynth: Information-Guided Benchmark Synthesis for LLMs
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant advancements in reasoning and code generation, but efficiently creating new benchmarks to evaluate these capabilities remains a challenge. Traditional benchmark creation relies on manual human effort, which is expensive and time-consuming. Furthermore, existing benchmarks often contaminate LLM training data, necessitating novel and diverse benchmarks to accurately assess their genuine capabilities. This work introduces InfoSynth, a novel framework for automatically generating and evaluating reasoning benchmarks guided by information-theoretic principles. We propose metrics based on KL-divergence and entropy to quantify benchmark novelty and diversity without relying on costly model evaluations. Building on this framework, we develop an end-to-end pipeline that synthesizes robust Python coding problems from seed datasets using genetic algorithms and iterative code feedback. Our method generates accurate test cases and solutions to new problems 97% of the time, and the synthesized benchmarks consistently exhibit higher difficulty compared to prior works. Moreover, our algorithm provides a method for controlling the novelty/diversity and difficulty of generated problems. InfoSynth offers a scalable, self-verifying pipeline for constructing high-quality, challenging coding benchmarks for LLMs. Project Page: https://ishirgarg.github.io/infosynth_web/

light_analysis.overview: 提出InfoSynth框架，基于信息论自动生成高质量、多样化的LLM推理基准，无需人工标注。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有基准创建依赖昂贵的人工，且易污染LLM训练数据，难以准确评估模型真实能力。需要一种自动化、可扩展的方法生成新颖且多样化的基准，以衡量LLM在推理和代码生成方面的进步。
light_analysis.method: 利用KL散度和熵定义新颖性与多样性指标，无需模型评估。通过种子数据集和遗传算法结合迭代代码反馈，合成Python编程问题。自动生成测试用例和解决方案，并允许控制基准的难度与多样性。
light_analysis.result: 方法生成准确测试用例和解决方案的成功率达97%。合成的基准在难度上持续高于先前工作，证明可生成更具挑战性的评估任务。
light_analysis.conclusion: InfoSynth提供可扩展、自验证的流水线，用于构建高质量编程基准，有助于更公平评估LLM。未来可扩展至其他推理领域，但需注意合成问题的领域覆盖度。

title: No Data? No Problem: Robust Vision-Tabular Learning with Missing Values
abstract: Large-scale medical biobanks provide imaging data complemented by extensive tabular information, such as clinical measurements or demographics. However, this abundance of tabular attributes does not reflect real-world datasets, where only a subset of attributes may be available. This discrepancy calls for methods that remain robust to missing values at inference. To address this challenge, we propose RoVTL (Robust Vision-Tabular Learning), a framework designed to handle any level of tabular data availability, from 0% to 100%. RoVTL comprises two key stages: contrastive pretraining, where we introduce tabular attribute missingness as data augmentation to promote robustness, and downstream task tuning, where tabular missingness is complemented by a novel Tabular More vs. Fewer loss that ranks performance based on the amount of available tabular data. Combined with gated-cross attention fusion module, our tuning approach enables consistent performance across all tabular data completeness scenarios. We evaluate RoVTL on cardiac MRI scans from the UK Biobank, demonstrating superior robustness to missing tabular data compared to prior methods. Furthermore, RoVTL successfully generalizes to an external cardiac MRI dataset for multimodal disease classification, and extends to the natural images domain, achieving robust performance on a car advertisements dataset. The model weights and code are available at https://github.com/marteczkah/RoVTL.

light_analysis.overview: 提出RoVTL框架，通过将表格属性缺失视为数据增强并引入新的损失函数，实现视觉-表格学习中对任意缺失比例的鲁棒性。
light_analysis.motivation: 大规模生物库提供丰富的表格属性，但实际数据集往往只有部分属性可用，现有方法对测试时缺失值不够鲁棒。需要一种能处理从0%到100%表格数据可用性的方法，以弥合理想与现实之间的差距。
light_analysis.method: RoVTL包含两阶段：对比预训练中引入表格属性缺失作为数据增强以提升鲁棒性；下游任务调优中，采用Tabular More vs. Fewer损失根据可用表格数据量对性能排序，并结合门控交叉注意力融合模块，实现一致的全场景性能。
light_analysis.result: 在UK Biobank心脏MRI数据集上，RoVTL相比先前方法对缺失表格数据表现出更强的鲁棒性；成功泛化到外部心脏MRI数据集和自然图像领域（汽车广告数据集），代码已开源。
light_analysis.conclusion: RoVTL有效处理了从0%到100%表格数据缺失的挑战，在多领域任务中验证了鲁棒性，具有实际应用价值。未来工作可探索更复杂的缺失模式或扩展到更多模态。

title: A Logical View of GNN-Style Computation and the Role of Activation Functions
abstract: We study the numerical and Boolean expressiveness of MPLang, a declarative language that captures the computation of graph neural networks (GNNs) through linear message passing and activation functions. We begin with A-MPLang, the fragment without activation functions, and give a characterization of its expressive power in terms of walk-summed features. For bounded activation functions, we show that (under mild conditions) all eventually constant activations yield the same expressive power - numerical and Boolean - and that it subsumes previously established logics for GNNs with eventually constant activation functions but without linear layers. Finally, we prove the first expressive separation between unbounded and bounded activations in the presence of linear layers: MPLang with ReLU is strictly more powerful for numerical queries than MPLang with eventually constant activation functions, e.g., truncated ReLU. This hinges on subtle interactions between linear aggregation and eventually constant non-linearities, and it establishes that GNNs using ReLU are more expressive than those restricted to eventually constant activations and linear layers.

light_analysis.overview: 本文通过逻辑语言MPLang形式化分析GNN的表达能力，证明ReLU激活函数比有界激活函数能赋予GNN更强的数值查询表达能力。
light_analysis.motivation: GNN的表达能力是理解其局限性的核心问题。已有工作多关注布尔查询，而数值查询的表达性研究不足。激活函数（如ReLU与截断ReLU）如何影响数值表达能力尚不明确。本文旨在通过逻辑语言MPLang提供统一的视角，揭示线性层与不同激活函数相互作用对表达性的影响。
light_analysis.method: 定义声明式语言MPLang，包含线性消息传递和激活函数。将其分解为无激活函数的A-MPLang片段，用随机游走求和特征刻画其表达能力。对于有界激活函数，证明在温和条件下所有最终常数激活（如sigmoid、tanh、截断ReLU）在数值和布尔查询上表达能力等价，且包含先前针对GNN的递归逻辑。最后，通过构造反例证明，在线性层存在时，ReLU（无界激活）比任何最终常数激活更强大，严格区分了数值查询的表达性。
light_analysis.result: 主要结果为理论表达性分离：MPLang with ReLU在数值查询上严格强于MPLang with最终常数激活函数（如截断ReLU）。论文未提供实验数据或基准对比，但通过形式化证明建立了这一分离。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文从逻辑角度系统分析了GNN中激活函数的作用，揭示了线性层与无界激活（如ReLU）结合能提升数值表达能力，而最终常数激活则受限于较弱的表达能力。这一理论发现有助于指导GNN架构设计，暗示了ReLU等无界激活在需要精细数值推理任务中的优势。未来可扩展到其他无界激活或更复杂的消息传递机制。

title: How to Square Tensor Networks and Circuits Without Squaring Them
abstract: Squared tensor networks (TNs) and their extension as computational graphs--squared circuits--have been used as expressive distribution estimators, yet supporting closed-form marginalization. However, the squaring operation introduces additional complexity when computing the partition function or marginalizing variables, which hinders their applicability in ML. To solve this issue, canonical forms of TNs are parameterized via unitary matrices to simplify the computation of marginals. However, these canonical forms do not apply to circuits, as they can represent factorizations that do not directly map to a known TN. Inspired by the ideas of orthogonality in canonical forms and determinism in circuits enabling tractable maximization, we show how to parameterize squared circuits to overcome their marginalization overhead. Our parameterizations unlock efficient marginalization even in factorizations different from TNs, but encoded as circuits, whose structure would otherwise make marginalization computationally hard. Finally, our experiments on distribution estimation show how our proposed conditions in squared circuits come with no expressiveness loss, while enabling more efficient learning.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种参数化平方电路的方法，无需显式平方操作即可高效计算边际概率，同时保持模型表达力和学习效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 平方张量网络和电路作为分布估计器，虽支持闭式边际化，但平方操作引入额外计算开销，尤其在大规模应用中限制实用性。现有规范形式通过酉矩阵简化张量网络计算，但不适用于电路结构。亟需一种新方法，能针对电路的不同分解模式，在不增加计算复杂度前提下实现高效边际化。
light_analysis.method: 受规范形式中正交性和电路中确定性思想的启发，本文提出通过施加特定参数化条件（如正交性）来平方电路，从而避免显式计算平方张量。方法适用于编码为电路而非传统张量网络的分解，利用电路的结构特性高效计算边际概率，同时保持与原有平方模型相同的表达能力。
light_analysis.result: 在分布估计任务中，所提出的参数化平方电路与基线方法相比，实现了更高效的学习过程，且实验结果表明无表达能力损失。具体性能提升如训练时间减少或参数效率提高，摘要未提供精确数值，但强调在保持竞争性似然的同时降低了计算开销。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文解决了平方电路在边际化计算中的效率瓶颈，提出的参数化条件使得电路能像张量网络一样高效处理边际概率。该工作为概率模型在机器学习中的应用提供了新工具，尤其适用于需要精确推理的场景。未来可探索更复杂电路结构下的进一步优化及其在生成模型中的应用。

title: AdaSD: Adaptive Speculative Decoding for Efficient Language Model Inference
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable performance across a wide range of tasks, but their increasing parameter sizes significantly slow down inference. Speculative decoding mitigates this issue by leveraging a smaller draft model to predict candidate tokens, which are then verified by a larger target model. However, existing approaches often require additional training, extensive hyperparameter tuning, or prior analysis of models and tasks before deployment. In this paper, we propose Adaptive Speculative Decoding (AdaSD), a hyperparameter-free decoding scheme that dynamically adjusts generation length and acceptance criteria during inference. AdaSD introduces two adaptive components: one to determine when to stop candidate token generation and the other to decide token acceptance, updated in real time based on token entropy and Jensen-Shannon distance. This approach eliminates the need for pre-analysis or fine-tuning and is compatible with off-the-shelf models. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that AdaSD achieves up to 1.46x speedup over vanilla speculative decoding while limiting accuracy degradation to under 1.8%, making it a practical solution for efficient and adaptive LLM inference.

light_analysis.overview: 提出AdaSD，一种无需超参数的自适应推测解码方案，动态调整生成长度和接受标准，加速LLM推理。
light_analysis.motivation: 大型语言模型推理速度慢，现有推测解码方法需额外训练、超参数调优或预分析，部署成本高。AdaSD旨在消除这些限制，实现即插即用的高效推理。
light_analysis.method: AdaSD包含两个实时自适应组件：一个基于token熵决定何时停止候选生成，另一个基于Jensen-Shannon距离决定接受阈值。无需预先分析或微调，兼容现有模型。
light_analysis.result: 在基准数据集上，AdaSD相比普通推测解码实现最高1.46倍加速，同时精度损失低于1.8%，在效率和准确性之间取得良好平衡。
light_analysis.conclusion: AdaSD提供了一种实用、自适应的LLM推理加速方案，无需手动调整。未来可探索更复杂的自适应策略或扩展到其他模型架构。

title: LiM-YOLO: Less is More with Pyramid Level Shift for Ship Detection in Optical Remote Sensing
abstract: General-purpose object detectors face fundamental structural limitations when applied to ship detection in satellite imagery, where the ship scale distribution is concentrated at small sizes and high aspect ratios. In conventional You Only Look Once architectures, the deepest feature pyramid level (stride 32) compresses narrow vessels into sub-pixel representations, causing severe spatial feature dilution and compromising accurate ship boundary regression. We propose Less is More YOLO, a streamlined detector built upon the extra-large variant of YOLOv9, to address these domain-specific structural conflicts. From a statistical analysis of ship scale distributions across four major benchmarks (SODA-A, DOTA-v1.5, FAIR1M-v2.0, and ShipRSImageNet), we introduce a Pyramid Level Shift Strategy that shifts the detection head from strides 8, 16, and 32 to strides 4, 8, and 16. This shift satisfies a spatial representability condition derived from the Nyquist-Shannon principle for the narrowest targets, while eliminating the computational redundancy of the deepest pyramid level. To further stabilize training on high-resolution satellite inputs, we incorporate a group-normalized auxiliary projection module that introduces Group Normalization into the projection path, mitigating gradient instability in memory-constrained micro-batch regimes. Validated on these four datasets, our detector attains an mAP_{50-95} of 0.600 with only 21.16 million parameters, a 64.1% reduction from the extra-large YOLOv9 baseline (58.99 million). Despite this compact size, our model surpasses state-of-the-art detectors up to three times larger, validating that a well-targeted pyramid level shift achieves a "Less is More" balance between accuracy and efficiency. The code is available at https://github.com/egshkim/LiM-YOLO.

light_analysis.overview: 提出LiM-YOLO，通过金字塔层级移位策略和分组归一化辅助模块，在减少64.1%参数量的同时提升船舶检测精度。
light_analysis.motivation: 通用目标检测器在卫星图像船舶检测中面临结构限制：船舶尺度集中在小型且高长宽比，传统YOLO最深特征层（stride 32）将窄小船体压缩为亚像素表示，导致空间特征稀释和边界回归不准。现有方法未针对该领域分布优化，存在计算冗余与精度损失。
light_analysis.method: 基于YOLOv9超大变体，提出金字塔层级移位策略：将检测头从stride 8/16/32下移至4/8/16，满足奈奎斯特-香农采样定理对最窄目标的空间可表示性条件。同时引入组归一化辅助投影模块（Group-Normalized Auxiliary Projection），在投影路径加入组归一化，缓解高分辨率卫星输入下显存受限微批训练的梯度不稳定问题。
light_analysis.result: 在SODA-A、DOTA-v1.5、FAIR1M-v2.0和ShipRSImageNet四个数据集上，LiM-YOLO以21.16M参数（较YOLOv9超大基线减少64.1%）达到mAP50-95=0.600，超越三倍参数量的最先进检测器。
light_analysis.conclusion: 验证了针对船舶尺度分布的金字塔层级移位策略可实现“少即是多”的精度-效率平衡，为卫星图像小目标检测提供了轻量化方案。代码已开源，未来可扩展至其他细长目标场景。

title: Training-Free Vector Quantization via Gaussian VAEs
abstract: Vector-quantized variational autoencoders (VQ-VAEs) are discrete autoencoders that compress images into discrete tokens. However, they are difficult to train due to discretization. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective technique dubbed Gaussian Quant (GQ), which first trains a Gaussian VAE under certain constraints and then converts it into a VQ-VAE without additional training. For conversion, GQ generates random Gaussian noise as a codebook and finds the closest noise vector to the posterior mean. Theoretically, we prove that when the logarithm of the codebook size exceeds the bits-back coding rate of the Gaussian VAE, a small quantization error is guaranteed. Practically, we propose a heuristic to train Gaussian VAEs for effective conversion, named the target divergence constraint (TDC). Empirically, we show that GQ outperforms previous VQ-VAEs, such as VQGAN, FSQ, LFQ, and BSQ, on both UNet and ViT architectures. Furthermore, TDC also improves previous Gaussian VAE discretization methods, such as TokenBridge. The source code is provided in https://github.com/tongdaxu/VQ-VAE-from-Gaussian-VAE.

light_analysis.overview: 提出无需额外训练的向量量化方法Gaussian Quant，通过训练约束高斯VAE并转换为VQ-VAE，理论保证量化误差。
light_analysis.motivation: VQ-VAE通过离散编码压缩图像，但离散化过程导致训练困难。现有方法需要复杂训练技巧，限制了实用性。本文旨在设计一种简单有效的无需训练转换的方法，将高斯VAE转化为VQ-VAE，降低训练难度并提升性能。
light_analysis.method: 核心方法Gaussian Quant (GQ)：先训练满足目标散度约束（TDC）的高斯VAE，然后随机生成高斯噪声作为码本，将后验均值映射到最近噪声向量完成量化。理论证明码本大小超过bits-back编码率时量化误差有界。TDC通过约束先验与后验散度提升转换效果。
light_analysis.result: 在UNet和ViT架构上，GQ在图像重建和生成任务中优于VQGAN、FSQ、LFQ、BSQ等VQ-VAE方法。TDC还改进了TokenBridge等高斯VAE离散化方法。具体数值摘要未明确，但声称整体性能领先。
light_analysis.conclusion: GQ提供了一种新颖的无需训练的VQ-VAE转换范式，理论分析与实验验证了其有效性。TDC可独立提升高斯VAE离散化。未来可探索其他先验分布或码本构造方式。代码已开源。

title: Mechanistic Interpretability of Antibody Language Models Using SAEs
abstract: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are a mechanistic interpretability technique that have been used to provide insight into learned concepts within large protein language models. Here, we employ TopK and Ordered SAEs to investigate autoregressive antibody language models, and steer their generation. We show that TopK SAEs can reveal biologically meaningful latent features, but high feature-concept correlation does not guarantee causal control over generation. In contrast, Ordered SAEs impose a hierarchical structure that reliably identifies steerable features, but at the expense of more complex and less interpretable activation patterns. These findings advance the mechanistic interpretability of domain-specific protein language models and suggest that, while TopK SAEs suffice for mapping latent features to concepts, Ordered SAEs are preferable when precise generative steering is required.
comment: v3: 15 pages; corrected author list and affiliations in the main text; minor text changes; updated steering results following minor code changes; conclusions and findings remain unchanged; included link to data and code in the Data Availability section

light_analysis.overview: 对比TopK和Ordered稀疏自编码器在抗体语言模型中的机械可解释性应用。
light_analysis.motivation: 大型蛋白质语言模型内部概念学习机制不透明，现有稀疏自编码器（SAEs）虽能揭示潜在特征，但缺乏对特征因果控制能力的系统评估。本文旨在探究不同SAE变体在抗体语言模型中的可解释性和生成引导能力，以填补领域空白。
light_analysis.method: 采用TopK和Ordered稀疏自编码器分析自回归抗体语言模型。TopK SAEs通过稀疏激活揭示生物概念相关特征，但相关性不等同于因果控制；Ordered SAEs引入层次结构，强制特征有序排列，提升可引导性但增加激活模式复杂度。实验基于抗体序列数据集。
light_analysis.result: TopK SAEs成功映射特征到概念（如抗体区域），但高相关性特征无法稳定控制生成；Ordered SAEs虽激活更复杂，但能可靠识别可引导特征，实现精准生成调控。结果表明两者适用场景不同。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文推进领域特定蛋白质语言模型的机械可解释性，明确TopK SAEs适用于特征映射，Ordered SAEs更优用于生成引导。未来可探索结合两者优势的混合方法，并扩展至其他生物序列模型。

title: SEAL: Self-Evolving Agentic Learning for Conversational Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs
abstract: Knowledge-based conversational question answering (KBCQA) confronts persistent challenges in resolving coreference, modeling contextual dependencies, and executing complex logical reasoning. Existing approaches often suffer from inaccuracies and prohibitive computational costs, particularly when processing intricate queries over large knowledge graphs. Specifically, large language models (LLMs) tend to generate syntactically invalid or semantically misaligned logical forms for complex multi-hop or aggregation queries, while conventional entity-relation linking methods face an exponentially growing candidate space.   To address these limitations, we introduce SEAL, a novel two-stage semantic parsing framework grounded in self-evolving agentic learning. In the first stage, an LLM extracts a minimal S-expression core capturing the essential semantics, which is then refined by an agentic calibration module to correct syntactic inconsistencies and align entities and relations with the knowledge graph. The second stage employs template-based completion guided by question-type prediction to construct a fully executable S-expression. Crucially, SEAL incorporates a self-evolving mechanism integrating local and global memory with a reflection module, enabling continuous adaptation from dialog history and execution feedback without explicit retraining. Extensive experiments on the SPICE benchmark demonstrate that SEAL achieves state-of-the-art performance in multi-hop reasoning, comparison, and aggregation tasks, validating notable gains in both structural accuracy and computational efficiency.

light_analysis.overview: 提出SEAL框架，通过自进化智能体学习实现知识图谱会话问答的两阶段语义解析，在多跳推理等任务上取得SOTA。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有方法在知识图谱会话问答中处理指代消解、上下文依赖和复杂逻辑推理时存在不准确和高计算成本问题。大语言模型生成的逻辑形式常存在语法或语义偏差，传统实体关系链接方法面临候选空间指数增长，亟需高效准确的解析框架。
light_analysis.method: SEAL采用两阶段语义解析：第一阶段由LLM提取最小S-expression核心语义，经智能体校准模块修正语法并与知识图谱对齐；第二阶段基于问题类型预测的模板补全生成可执行S-expression。核心创新是自进化机制，结合局部/全局记忆与反思模块，通过对话历史和执行反馈持续适应，无需显式重训练。
light_analysis.result: 在SPICE基准上的实验表明，SEAL在多跳推理、比较和聚合任务中取得最先进性能，结构准确率和计算效率均有显著提升。摘要未明确具体数值，但强调相比现有方法在准确性和效率上的双重优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: SEAL框架有效解决了知识图谱会话问答中的核心挑战，通过自进化学习避免了重训练需求，兼具高性能与低计算成本。未来可扩展至更复杂的逻辑形式或跨领域应用，当前方法在极端复杂查询上的表现有待进一步验证。

title: Unique Lives, Shared World: Learning from Single-Life Videos
abstract: We introduce the "single-life" learning paradigm, where we train a distinct vision model exclusively on egocentric videos captured by one individual. We leverage the multiple viewpoints naturally captured within a single life to learn a visual encoder in a self-supervised manner. Our experiments demonstrate three key findings. First, models trained independently on different lives develop a highly aligned geometric understanding. We demonstrate this by training visual encoders on distinct datasets each capturing a different life, both indoors and outdoors, as well as introducing a novel cross-attention-based metric to quantify the functional alignment of the internal representations developed by different models. Second, we show that single-life models learn generalizable geometric representations that effectively transfer to downstream tasks, such as depth estimation, in unseen environments. Third, we demonstrate that training on up to 30 hours from one week of the same person's life leads to comparable performance to training on 30 hours of diverse web data, highlighting the strength of single-life representation learning. Overall, our results establish that the shared structure of the world, both leads to consistency in models trained on individual lives, and provides a powerful signal for visual representation learning.

light_analysis.overview: 提出单生命周期学习范式，利用个人第一人称视频自监督训练视觉编码器，发现模型间几何对齐且迁移能力强大。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统视觉模型依赖大规模多样数据，但个体的独特视角能否学到通用表示尚不明确。现有方法未充分利用单个人连续视频中的多视角信息。该研究旨在证明：世界的共享结构可使个体模型产生一致性几何理解，从而减少对海量数据的依赖。
light_analysis.method: 提出“单独生命”学习范式，在单一人第一人称视频上训练视觉编码器。利用自监督学习，从不同时间点的多视角中提取几何表示。引入交叉注意力指标量化不同模型内部表示的几何对齐程度。数据集包含室内外不同个体的30小时视频。
light_analysis.result: 实验发现：1）不同个体训练的模型具有高度对齐的几何理解；2）单生命周期模型在未见环境的深度估计等下游任务中表现良好；3）30小时单个人视频性能与30小时多样网络数据相当，体现了单生命周期表示学习的强大。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究证明世界的共享结构使单个人视频训练的模型产生一致性，为视觉表示学习提供新范式。学术上挑战了数据规模驱动的假设，实际中可应用于个性化模型或隐私保护场景。未来可能扩展至更多任务或长视频理解。

title: Reconstructing Multi-Scale Physical Fields from Extremely Sparse Measurements with an Autoencoder-Diffusion Cascade
abstract: Extreme sensor sparsity makes full-field reconstruction a fundamentally ill-posed problem in scientific sensing,where the goal is to infer physical fields from sparse measurements.In this regime,the posterior is severely underconstrained and inherently multimodal,making its approximation highly ill-conditioned.Specifically,deterministic mappings collapse uncertainty,direct conditional learning cannot cover the space of possible observation-conditioned solutions,and likelihood-guided sampling becomes highly sensitive to noise and sensor configurations.These limitations result in unstable posterior estimates and highlight the need for modeling uncertainty in a structural manner.To this end,we propose Cascaded Sensing,a hierarchical framework that restructures posterior inference across scales.Rather than modeling the full-field posterior directly,Cas-Sensing first resolves global structural ambiguity through a deterministic coarse-stage estimator.A neural-operator-based functional autoencoder,trained with masked inputs,maps sparse observations to a coarse-scale structural field,acting analogously to a maximum a posteriori estimator that selects the dominant global configuration.This structural anchor fixes the principal degrees of freedom of the posterior and transforms the problem into a better-conditioned residual inference task.A conditional diffusion model then learns only the refined-scale residual distribution,confining sampling to a stable neighborhood of plausible solutions and suppressing competition among observation-consistent modes.To enhance robustness under varying sensing conditions,we introduce mask-cascade training,which exposes the model to diverse sparse observation patterns through intermediate coarse reconstructions.During inference,manifold-constrained guidance enforces observation consistency as a refinement mechanism rather than a global mode-selection process.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Cascaded Sensing框架，通过自编码器-扩散级联从极端稀疏测量中重建多尺度物理场。
light_analysis.motivation: 极端传感器稀疏性导致全场重构成为严重病态问题，后验分布严重欠约束且固有模态，现有方法如确定性映射会坍缩不确定性，直接条件学习无法覆盖所有可能解，似然引导采样对噪声和传感器配置高度敏感，导致后验估计不稳定，亟需结构性不确定性建模。
light_analysis.method: 提出Cascaded Sensing层次框架，先通过粗尺度估计器（基于掩码输入训练的神经算子功能自编码器，类似MAP估计）解析全局结构模糊性，得到结构锚点；再通过条件扩散模型学习精细尺度残差分布，将采样限制在稳定邻域。引入掩码级联训练增强对多样稀疏模式的鲁棒性，推理时使用流形约束引导作为细化机制。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确说明具体实验数据，但指出该方法通过分解后验推理为确定性粗估计和残差扩散，解决了直接建模全尺度后验的不稳定性，实现了更鲁棒的重构。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出Cascaded Sensing框架，通过层次化后验推理有效处理极端稀疏测量下的多模态不确定性，具有理论意义和实际应用价值，但摘要未提及局限性及未来工作。

title: LEC: Linear Expectation Constraints for Selection-Conditioned Risk Control in Selective Prediction and Routing Systems
abstract: Foundation models often generate unreliable answers, while heuristic uncertainty estimators fail to fully distinguish correct from incorrect outputs, causing users to accept erroneous answers without any statistical guarantee. We address this problem through selection-conditioned risk control, aiming to ensure that an accepted prediction has an error probability no larger than a user-specified risk level. To this end, we propose LEC, a principled framework that reframes selective prediction as a decision problem governed by a linear expectation constraint over selection and error indicators. This formulation directly controls the ratio between the expected number of accepted errors and the expected number of accepted predictions, which corresponds to the marginal error probability conditioned on selection. Under exchangeability, we derive a finite-sample sufficient condition that relies only on a held-out calibration set, enabling the computation of a risk-constrained, retention-maximizing threshold. Furthermore, we extend LEC to two-model routing systems: if the primary model's uncertainty exceeds its calibrated threshold, the input is delegated to a subsequent model, while maintaining system-level selection-conditioned error control. Experiments on both closed-ended and open-ended question answering (QA) and vision question answering (VQA) demonstrate that LEC maintains the prescribed risk level in accepted predictions and substantially improves sample retention compared to baselines.

light_analysis.overview: 提出LEC框架，通过线性期望约束实现选择条件风险控制，确保接受预测的错误概率不超过用户指定风险水平，并扩展至两模型路由系统。
light_analysis.motivation: 基础模型常产生不可靠答案，启发式不确定性估计无法完全区分正确与错误输出，导致用户接受错误答案而无统计保证。需要一种有统计保证的方法来控制接受预测的误差风险，确保用户指定风险水平。
light_analysis.method: LEC将选择性预测重新定义为受线性期望约束的决策问题，控制接受错误期望与接受预测期望之比（选择条件边际误差概率）。基于可交换性，推导出有限样本充分条件，使用校准集计算风险约束且保留最大化的阈值。扩展至两模型路由系统：当主模型不确定性超过阈值时，输入交由后续模型，同时保持系统级选择条件误差控制。
light_analysis.result: 在闭式和开放式问答（QA）及视觉问答（VQA）任务上，LEC能维持用户指定的风险水平，并相比基线方法显著提高样本保留率，即在控制风险前提下接受更多样本。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文提出LEC框架，在选择性预测和路由系统中实现统计保证的风险控制。学术上提供基于线性期望约束的新视角，实际应用可提升AI系统可靠性，避免用户接受错误答案。

title: PaTAS: A Framework for Trust Propagation in Neural Networks Using Subjective Logic
abstract: Trustworthiness has become a key requirement for the deployment of artificial intelligence systems in safety-critical applications. Conventional evaluation metrics, such as accuracy and precision, fail to appropriately capture uncertainty or the reliability of model predictions, particularly under adversarial or degraded conditions. This paper introduces the Parallel Trust Assessment System (PaTAS), a framework for modeling and propagating trust in neural networks using Subjective Logic (SL). PaTAS operates in parallel with standard neural computation through Trust Nodes and Trust Functions that propagate input, parameter, and activation trust across the network. The framework defines a Parameter Trust Update mechanism to refine parameter reliability during training and an Inference-Path Trust Assessment (IPTA) method to compute instance-specific trust at inference. Experiments on real-world and adversarial datasets demonstrate that PaTAS produces interpretable, symmetric, and convergent trust estimates that complement accuracy and expose reliability gaps in poisoned, biased, or uncertain data scenarios. The results show that PaTAS effectively distinguishes between benign and adversarial inputs and identifies cases where model confidence diverges from actual reliability. By enabling transparent and quantifiable trust reasoning within neural architectures, PaTAS provides a foundation for evaluating model reliability across the AI lifecycle.

light_analysis.overview: 提出PaTAS框架，利用主观逻辑在神经网络中并行传播信任，以量化模型可靠性。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统评估指标（如准确率、精确率）无法充分捕捉不确定性和模型预测的可靠性，尤其在对抗性或退化条件下。现有方法缺乏对信任的透明量化和传播机制，难以解释模型在安全关键应用中的真实可信度。
light_analysis.method: PaTAS通过信任节点和信任函数与标准神经计算并行运行，传播输入、参数和激活信任。定义参数信任更新机制以在训练中精炼参数可靠性，以及推理路径信任评估方法在推理时计算实例特定信任。
light_analysis.result: 在真实和对抗数据集上，PaTAS产生可解释、对称且收敛的信任估计，补充准确性指标并暴露可靠性差距。有效区分良性输入和对抗输入，识别模型置信度与实际可靠性不一致的情况。
light_analysis.conclusion: PaTAS提供了在神经架构内进行透明量化信任推理的框架，为AI生命周期中模型可靠性评估奠定基础。未来可扩展至更复杂的网络结构和动态信任更新。

title: From Per-Image Low-Rank to Encoding Mismatch: Rethinking Feature Distillation in Vision Transformers
abstract: Feature-map knowledge distillation (KD) transfers internal representations well between comparably sized Vision Transformers (ViTs), but it often fails in compression. We revisit this failure and uncover a paradox. Sample-wise SVD shows that each image is highly compressible, which seems to suggest that a narrow student with a linear projector should match the teacher "in principle". However, a dataset-level view contradicts this intuition: PCA shows that the teacher is a union of low-rank subspaces with significant subspace rotation across inputs.
