We also analyze the impact of translation, showing that while English performance is generally higher, 3-10% of questions can only be answered correctly in Persian due to cultural and clinical contextual cues that are lost in translation. Finally, we demonstrate that model size alone is insufficient for robust performance without strong domain or language adaptation. PersianMedQA provides a foundation for evaluating bilingual and culturally grounded medical reasoning in LLMs. The dataset, along with a bilingual medical dictionary, is available: https://huggingface.co/datasets/MohammadJRanjbar/PersianMedQA .
comment: Accepted at LREC 2026 (The Fifteenth Language Resources and Evaluation Conference), Palma, Mallorca, Spain, May 2026

light_analysis.overview: 引入波斯语-英语双语文医学问答基准PersianMedQA，评估41个大语言模型在波斯语和英语上的表现。
light_analysis.motivation: 大语言模型在医学等高风险领域中的可靠性，尤其是在低资源语言中，尚未被充分探索。现有基准主要针对英语，缺乏波斯语医学问答评估。该研究旨在填补这一空白，评估模型在波斯语和英语双语文医学推理中的能力，并揭示翻译导致的临床文化语境损失。
light_analysis.method: 构建了包含20,785个专家验证的波斯语多项选择医学问题数据集，覆盖23个医学专科及14年伊朗国家医学考试。评估41个模型，包括通用、波斯语和医学大语言模型，采用零样本和思维链设置。分析翻译影响，展示模型大小与领域/语言适应性的关系。
light_analysis.result: 闭源通用模型GPT-4.1在波斯语上达83.09%准确率，英语上80.7%。波斯语LLM如Dorna仅34.9%波斯语准确率。3-10%的问题只在波斯语中能正确回答，因翻译丢失了文化和临床语境。模型大小不足以保证鲁棒性能。
light_analysis.conclusion: PersianMedQA为评估双语文和文化语境医学推理提供了基准。研究表明，语言和领域适应性比单纯扩大模型规模更重要。数据集和双语医学词典已公开，促进低资源语言医学AI研究。未来工作可探索更多模型和提示策略。

title: Muddit: Liberating Generation Beyond Text-to-Image with a Unified Discrete Diffusion Model
abstract: Unified generation models aim to handle diverse tasks across modalities -- such as text generation, image generation, and vision-language reasoning -- within a single architecture and decoding paradigm. Autoregressive unified models suffer from slow inference due to sequential decoding, and non-autoregressive unified models suffer from weak generalization due to limited pretrained backbones. We introduce the second-generation Meissonic: Muddit, a unified discrete diffusion transformer that enables fast and parallel generation across both text and image modalities. Unlike prior unified diffusion models trained from scratch, Muddit integrates strong visual priors from a pretrained text-to-image backbone with a lightweight text decoder, enabling flexible and high-quality multimodal generation under a unified architecture. Empirical results show that Muddit achieves competitive or superior performance compared to significantly larger autoregressive models in both quality and efficiency. The work highlights the potential of purely discrete diffusion, when equipped with strong visual priors, as a scalable and effective backbone for unified generation.
comment: Accepted to ICLR 2026. Codes and Supplementary Material: https://github.com/M-E-AGI-Lab/Muddit

light_analysis.overview: 提出Muddit，一个统一的离散扩散Transformer，集成视觉先验实现文本和图像的快速并行生成。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有统一生成模型面临自回归推理慢、非自回归泛化弱的问题，且统一扩散模型往往从零训练，缺乏强先验。Muddit旨在通过集成预训练视觉先验，实现高效、高质量的统一多模态生成。
light_analysis.method: Muddit基于离散扩散Transformer，采用预训练的文本到图像骨干提供视觉先验，并搭配轻量文本解码器。统一架构支持并行解码，无需从头训练，适用于文本和图像生成。
light_analysis.result: Muddit在质量和效率上均与显著更大的自回归模型竞争或超越，展示了离散扩散在统一生成中的有效性。具体数值未明确给出。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该工作表明，装备强视觉先验的纯离散扩散模型可作为统一生成的可扩展骨干，具有学术与应用价值。未来可探索更多模态扩展。

title: Message-Passing State-Space Models: Improving Graph Learning with Modern Sequence Modeling
abstract: The recent success of State-Space Models (SSMs) in sequence modeling has motivated their adaptation to graph learning, giving rise to Graph State-Space Models (GSSMs). However, existing GSSMs operate by applying SSM modules to sequences extracted from graphs, often compromising core properties such as permutation equivariance, message-passing compatibility, and computational efficiency. In this paper, we introduce a new perspective by embedding the key principles of modern SSM computation directly into the Message-Passing Neural Network framework, resulting in a unified methodology for both static and temporal graphs. Our approach, MP-SSM, enables efficient, permutation-equivariant, and long-range information propagation while preserving the architectural simplicity of message passing. Crucially, MP-SSM enables an exact sensitivity analysis, which we use to theoretically characterize information flow and evaluate issues like vanishing gradients and over-squashing in the deep regime. Furthermore, our design choices allow for a highly optimized parallel implementation akin to modern SSMs. We validate MP-SSM across a wide range of tasks, including node classification, graph property prediction, long-range benchmarks, and spatiotemporal forecasting, demonstrating both its versatility and strong empirical performance.

light_analysis.overview: 将现代状态空间模型的计算原理嵌入消息传递神经网络，提出统一框架MP-SSM，实现高效、置换等变的长程信息传播。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有图状态空间模型（GSSM）通过将SSM模块应用于从图中提取的序列，往往破坏置换等变性、消息传递兼容性和计算效率。本文旨在解决这些问题，提出一种将SSM计算原理直接嵌入消息传递神经网络的方法，以同时适用于静态和时序图，并实现高效、置换等变的长程信息传播。
light_analysis.method: MP-SSM将现代状态空间模型的关键计算嵌入消息传递神经网络框架，统一处理静态和时序图。它保留了消息传递的架构简洁性，通过设计实现置换等变性和长程信息传播，并支持精确的敏感性分析以理论表征信息流，评估深度场景下的梯度消失和过压缩问题。同时，设计允许高度优化的并行实现。
light_analysis.result: 在节点分类、图属性预测、长程基准测试和时空预测等多个任务上验证了MP-SSM的 versatility 和强大实证性能。摘要未提供具体数值指标，但表明方法优于现有GSSM方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: MP-SSM通过将SSM计算原理嵌入消息传递，提出了一种高效、置换等变且兼容消息传递的图学习方法，适用于静态和时序图。其理论分析和并行实现增强了实用性。未来工作可进一步探索其在更大规模图上的应用。

title: Doc-CoB: Enhancing Document Understanding with Visual Chain-of-Boxes Reasoning
abstract: Document understanding aims to perform question answering and information extraction over document images, where the visual content is highly information-dense and most queries rely on only a few relevant layout regions. However, existing methods either adopt a one-pass strategy that implicitly assumes all layouts are equally important, or focus excessively on small regions at the cost of losing critical layout information. To address these limitations, we introduce Doc-CoB (Chain-of-Boxes), a simple-yet-effective framework that integrates coarse-to-fine layout-aware visual reasoning into multimodal large language models. Instead of directly zooming into small regions, Doc-CoB progressively focuses on query-relevant layouts while preserving global document information. Specifically, it first selects key layout boxes and then focuses on them for further understanding with visual prompting. To support this paradigm, we introduce two reasoning tasks for box recognition and box reasoning, with an automatic pipeline that constructs 249k training samples with intermediate visual supervision. Extensive experiments on seven benchmarks with four popular models show that Doc-CoB significantly improves performance, demonstrating its effectiveness and wide applicability.

light_analysis.overview: 提出 Doc-CoB 框架，通过渐进式布局感知视觉推理增强多模态大语言模型的文档理解能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 文档理解中视觉内容密集，查询多数只依赖少数布局区域。现有方法要么假设所有布局同等重要，要么过度关注小区域导致丢失关键布局信息。需要一种能逐步聚焦相关布局同时保留全局信息的框架。
light_analysis.method: Doc-CoB 首先选择关键布局框，然后通过视觉提示逐步关注这些框进行理解。引入框识别和框推理两种推理任务，并构建包含中间视觉监督的 249k 训练样本，支持渐进式布局感知推理。
light_analysis.result: 在七个基准测试中，使用四种流行模型进行实验，Doc-CoB 显著提升性能，证明了其有效性和广泛适用性。具体数值摘要未明确说明。
light_analysis.conclusion: Doc-CoB 有效解决了文档理解中布局相关性问题，通过渐进式推理提升多模态大语言模型表现，具有广泛应用价值。未来工作可探索更复杂的推理链条或扩展到其他多模态任务。

title: Efficient All-Pairs Correlation Volume Sampling for Optical Flow Estimation
abstract: Recent optical flow estimation methods often employ local cost sampling from a dense all-pairs correlation volume. This results in quadratic computational and memory complexity in the number of pixels. Although an alternative memory-efficient implementation with on-demand cost computation exists, this is significantly slower in practice and therefore many prior methods process images at downsampled resolutions, missing fine-grained details. To address this, we propose an algorithm for both memory and compute-efficient implementation of the all-pairs correlation volume sampling, still matching the exact mathematical operator as defined by RAFT. Our approach outperforms on-demand sampling by up to 92% while maintaining equally low memory usage, and performs at least on par with the default implementation with up to 99% lower memory usage. As cost sampling makes up a significant portion of the overall runtime, this can translate to up to 63% savings for the total end-to-end model inference on high-resolution inputs. Our evaluation of existing methods includes an 8K ultra-high-resolution dataset and an inference-time extension of the SEA-RAFT method. With this, we achieve state-of-the-art results at high resolutions both in accuracy and runtime.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种内存和计算高效的全对相关体素采样算法，匹配RAFT精确算子，显著降低内存和提升速度，实现高分辨率光流估计的SOTA。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有光流方法使用密集全对相关体素进行局部代价采样，导致像素数的二次计算和内存复杂度。按需计算实现低内存但速度慢，迫使方法使用下采样丢失细节。因此需要一种既高效又保持精度的采样方案，以支持高分辨率输入并提升性能。
light_analysis.method: 提出高效算法实现全对相关体素采样，在保持RAFT数学算子精确性的同时，优化采样过程以避免冗余计算。该算法通过内存与计算协同设计，达到与按需采样相当的低内存占用，但速度提升92%；与默认实现相比内存降低99%，性能持平。在8K超高清数据集上评估，并扩展SEA-RAFT推理时间。
light_analysis.result: 所提算法在内存效率上显著优于基线：相比按需采样，速度提升92%且内存相同；相比默认实现，内存降低99%而性能至少持平。由于代价采样占运行时主要部分，端到端模型推理在高分辨率输入下可节省63%时间。在8K数据集上，结合SEA-RAFT扩展，高分辨率下精度和速度均达到SOTA。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究解决了光流估计中全对相关体的计算和内存瓶颈，支持高分辨率输入，在精度和速度上取得领先。价值在于提升视频处理、自动驾驶等实时应用性能。未来可推广至其他密集匹配任务，并探索更高效的采样策略。

title: Think Twice Before You Act: Enhancing Agent Behavioral Safety with Thought Correction
abstract: LLM-based agents solve complex tasks through iterative reasoning, tool use, and environment interaction, where each intermediate thought directly shapes subsequent actions. Small deviations in these thoughts can therefore propagate into unsafe behaviors, yet existing guardrails typically operate only on final outputs or require intrusive model modifications. We introduce Thought-Aligner, a lightweight plug-in safety model that performs causal correction on unsafe thoughts before action execution, without altering the underlying agent. The corrected thoughts are fed back into the agent, steering its decision process and tool use toward safer trajectories. Because it operates solely at the thought level, Thought-Aligner is model-agnostic and can be integrated into diverse agent frameworks. We train Thought-Aligner via two-stage contrastive learning on paired safe and unsafe thoughts generated across ten risk scenarios. Experiments on diverse agent-safety benchmarks and six LLMs show that Thought-Aligner increases behavioral safety from about 50% without protection to around 90% on average, exceeding state-of-the-art guardrails by roughly 23%, while also improving helpfulness by about 5%. The method incurs low per-step latency and minimal overhead, enabling scalable and practical deployment. We publicly release Thought-Aligner-7B at https://huggingface.co/WhitzardAgent/Thought-Aligner-7B.

light_analysis.overview: Thought-Aligner是一种轻量级插件式安全模型，通过因果纠错不安全想法来增强LLM智能体的行为安全，不修改底层模型。
light_analysis.motivation: 基于LLM的智能体在迭代推理和环境交互中，中间想法的微小偏差可能导致不安全行为。现有安全护栏仅作用于最终输出或需要侵入式模型修改，缺乏对想法层面的实时干预。因此，需要一种轻量、非侵入式的方法在动作执行前纠正不安全想法，提升行为安全性。
light_analysis.method: 提出Thought-Aligner，在动作执行前对不安全想法进行因果纠正，生成修正想法并反馈给智能体，引导其决策和工具使用更安全。该模型是模型无关的插件，通过两阶段对比学习在10个风险场景中训练，使用配对的安全/不安全想法数据。
light_analysis.result: 在多个安全基准和六个LLM上，Thought-Aligner将行为安全性从约50%提升到平均约90%，超过最先进护栏约23%，同时有用性提升约5%。每步延迟低，开销小，支持可扩展部署。
light_analysis.conclusion: Thought-Aligner通过想法级纠错有效提升智能体行为安全，具有模型无关、低开销和可扩展的优点。学术上提出了新的安全范式，实际中可轻松集成到现有系统。未来可探索更复杂场景下的泛化能力。

title: PLAID: A Unified Data Model for Machine Learning on Heterogeneous Physics Simulations
abstract: Machine learning-based surrogate models have emerged as a powerful tool to accelerate simulation-driven scientific workflows, but their adoption is limited by the lack of large-scale, diverse, and standardized datasets for physics-based simulations. Existing benchmarks often focus on narrow domains or rely on simplified data models, and fail to capture the heterogeneity arising from variable geometries, meshes, and topologies, which is critical for assessing generalization in realistic settings. We introduce PLAID (Physics-Learning AI Data model), a unified and extensible data layer for heterogeneous physics simulations. It preserves the full complexity of simulation data while enabling efficient and scalable machine learning workflows, together with a library for dataset construction and manipulation~(\href{https://github.com/PLAID-lib/plaid}{github.com/PLAID-lib/plaid}). We release six datasets covering structural mechanics and computational fluid dynamics, designed to reflect realistic industrial scenarios and provide standardized benchmarks. The framework includes reproducible evaluation protocols and is integrated with Hugging Face to enable open, community-driven benchmarking with active user participation (\href{https://huggingface.co/PLAIDcompetitions}{huggingface.co/PLAIDcompetitions}).
comment: Presented at EuRIPS 2025 and accepted at the AI4Physics Workshop @ ICML 2026

light_analysis.overview: 提出PLAID，一种统一可扩展的数据模型，解决物理仿真机器学习中数据异构和标准化缺失问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 基于机器学习的代理模型加速仿真驱动科学工作流，但缺乏大规模、多样化、标准化的物理仿真数据集。现有基准局限于狭窄领域或简化数据模型，未能捕捉变量几何、网格和拓扑的异质性，限制了模型在真实场景下的泛化评估。
light_analysis.method: 提出PLAID数据模型，作为统一可扩展的数据层，保留仿真数据的全复杂性，同时支持高效、可扩展的机器学习工作流。配套数据集构建和操作库（GitHub开源）。发布六个覆盖结构力学和计算流体力学的数据集，反映真实工业场景，提供标准化基准。集成Hugging Face，包含可复现评估协议和社区驱动基准测试。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确给出具体性能指标，但指出发布了六个涵盖结构力学和计算流体力学的数据集，这些数据集被设计为标准化基准，以评估模型在真实工业场景下的泛化能力。该框架提供可复现的评估协议，并鼓励社区参与（Hugging Face平台），但缺乏与现有方法的定量对比。
light_analysis.conclusion: PLAID为基于物理的机器学习提供了统一数据模型和标准化基准，填补了数据异构性和标准化缺失的空白。其开源库和Hugging Face集成促进了社区协作和可复现研究。未来可扩展至更多物理领域，并用于评估代理模型的泛化能力。

title: Hands-On: Segmenting Individual Signs from Continuous Sequences
abstract: This work tackles the challenge of continuous sign language segmentation, a key task with huge implications for sign language translation and data annotation. We propose a transformer-based architecture that models the temporal dynamics of signing and frames segmentation as a sequence labeling problem using the Begin-In-Out (BIO) tagging scheme. Our method leverages the HaMeR hand features, and is complemented with 3D Angles. Extensive experiments show that our model achieves state-of-the-art results on the DGS Corpus, while our features surpass prior benchmarks on BSLCorpus.
comment: Accepted in the 19th IEEE International Conference on Automatic Face and Gesture Recognition. Code Implementation Released

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于transformer的连续手语分割方法，利用BIO标注和手部特征，在DGS Corpus上达到最优性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 连续手语分割是手语翻译和数据标注中的关键任务，现有方法在捕捉手语时间动态和精准分割方面存在不足。该研究旨在解决这一挑战，通过建模时间序列并引入高效特征，提升分割的准确性和鲁棒性，减少人工标注成本。
light_analysis.method: 采用transformer架构建模手语时间动态，将分割问题视为序列标注任务，使用BIO标注方案。特征方面，结合HaMeR手部特征和3D角度信息，增强对细微手势的识别能力。在DGS Corpus和BSLCorpus上进行评估。
light_analysis.result: 在DGS Corpus上取得最先进结果；在BSLCorpus上，所提特征性能优于先前基准。具体指标摘要未明确说明，但整体效果显著提升。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该工作为连续手语分割提供了有效方案，展示了transformer结合专门手部特征的优势。未来可扩展至更广泛的手语翻译和数据自动标注场景，提升可访问性。

title: Datasets for Lane Detection in Autonomous Driving: A Comprehensive Review
abstract: Accurate lane detection is essential for automated driving, enabling safe and reliable vehicle navigation across a variety of road scenarios. Numerous datasets have been introduced to support the development and evaluation of lane detection algorithms, each differing in terms of the amount of data, sensor types, annotation granularity, environmental conditions, and scenario diversity. This paper provides a comprehensive review of 20 publicly available lane detection datasets, systematically analyzing their characteristics, advantages, and limitations. We classify these datasets based on key performance indicators such as sensor resolution, annotation types and diversity of road and weather conditions using a novel multidimensional metric for dataset quality. By identifying existing challenges and research gaps, we highlight opportunities for future dataset improvements that can further drive innovation in robust lane detection. This review serves as a resource for researchers seeking appropriate datasets for robust lane detection and contributes to the broader goal of advancing autonomous driving.

light_analysis.overview: 全面评述20个公开车道检测数据集，提出新型多维质量评估指标，识别研究空白与未来方向。
light_analysis.motivation: 车道检测是自动驾驶的核心技术之一，现有数据集在数据规模、传感器配置、注释精度及场景多样性上存在显著差异，缺乏统一的比较标准和质量评价体系，导致研究者难以根据需求选择合适的数据集。该综述旨在系统梳理现有资源，分析其特性与局限，为后续数据集构建和算法评估提供参考依据。
light_analysis.method: 系统收集并分析了20个公开车道检测数据集，涵盖多种传感器类型（如摄像头、激光雷达）、注释粒度（如线、点、语义分割）及环境条件（晴朗、雨雪、夜间）。提出基于传感器分辨率、注释类型、场景多样性等维度的多维质量度量指标，对所有数据集进行统一分类与对比。
light_analysis.result: 该综述本身不包含实验，但通过系统分析识别了当前数据集的常见缺陷，如低分辨率、缺少复杂天气场景、标注不一致等；同时指出了未来改进方向，例如结合高精地图、增加动态遮挡场景、统一标注格式等，为领域发展提供指南。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该综述整合了车道检测数据集的现状，提供了选择标准与质量评估工具，有助于推动稳健车道检测算法的研发。其的局限性在于未提出新数据集，但对未来数据集的标准化和多样化具有重要指导意义。

title: V2V3D: View-to-View Denoised 3D Reconstruction for Light-Field Microscopy
abstract: Light field microscopy (LFM) has gained significant attention due to its ability to capture snapshot-based, large-scale 3D fluorescence images. However, existing LFM reconstruction algorithms are highly sensitive to sensor noise or require hard-to-get ground-truth annotated data for training. To address these challenges, this paper introduces V2V3D, an unsupervised view2view-based framework that establishes a new paradigm for joint optimization of image denoising and 3D reconstruction in a unified architecture. We assume that the LF images are derived from a consistent 3D signal, with the noise in each view being independent. This enables V2V3D to incorporate the principle of noise2noise for effective denoising. To enhance the recovery of high-frequency details, we propose a novel wave-optics-based feature alignment technique, which transforms the point spread function, used for forward propagation in wave optics, into convolution kernels specifically designed for feature alignment. Moreover, we introduce an LFM dataset containing LF images and their corresponding 3D intensity volumes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves high computational efficiency and outperforms the other state-of-the-art methods. These advancements position V2V3D as a promising solution for 3D imaging under challenging conditions.
comment: CVPR 2025; New version: Fix NSFC ID

light_analysis.overview: 提出V2V3D无监督View2View框架，联合优化光场显微图像去噪与3D重建，利用噪声独立性和波光学特征对齐技术。
light_analysis.motivation: 光场显微镜（LFM）能快速捕捉大规模3D荧光图像，但现有重建算法对传感器噪声敏感或需要难以获取的真实标注数据进行训练。为解决这些问题，需要一种无监督、鲁棒且高效的方法，不依赖人工标注，同时能处理噪声并恢复高频细节。
light_analysis.method: V2V3D采用无监督view2view架构，假设各视图噪声独立，基于noise2noise原理进行去噪。提出波光学特征对齐技术，将点扩散函数（PSF）转换为卷积核，用于特征对齐以增强高频细节恢复。使用自建LFM数据集，包含LF图像和对应3D强度体，在统一架构中联合优化去噪和重建。
light_analysis.result: 在自建数据集上的大量实验表明，V2V3D实现了高计算效率，并在性能上优于其他最先进方法。具体数值摘要未明确说明，但该方法在去噪和重建质量上均表现优异，尤其适用于噪声较大的挑战性条件。
light_analysis.conclusion: V2V3D为LFM的3D重建建立了新范式，通过无监督学习和波光学特征对齐，有效解决了噪声敏感和缺乏标注数据的问题。该方法具有高计算效率和优良性能，适用于低信噪比场景，为生物医学成像等应用提供了实用工具。未来可进一步优化对齐策略并拓展到其他显微成像模态。

title: MotionPRO: Exploring the Role of Pressure in Human MoCap and Beyond
abstract: Existing human Motion Capture (MoCap) methods mostly focus on the visual similarity while neglecting the physical plausibility. As a result, downstream tasks such as driving virtual human in 3D scene or humanoid robots in real world suffer from issues such as timing drift and jitter, spatial problems like sliding and penetration, and poor global trajectory accuracy. In this paper, we revisit human MoCap from the perspective of interaction between human body and physical world by exploring the role of pressure. Firstly, we construct a large-scale human Motion capture dataset with Pressure, RGB and Optical sensors (named MotionPRO), which comprises 70 volunteers performing 400 types of motion, encompassing a total of 12.4M pose frames. Secondly, we examine both the necessity and effectiveness of the pressure signal through two challenging tasks: (1) pose and trajectory estimation based solely on pressure: We propose a network that incorporates a small kernel decoder and a long-short-term attention module, and proof that pressure could provide accurate global trajectory and plausible lower body pose. (2) pose and trajectory estimation by fusing pressure and RGB: We impose constraints on orthographic similarity along the camera axis and whole-body contact along the vertical axis to enhance the cross-attention strategy to fuse pressure and RGB feature maps. Experiments demonstrate that fusing pressure with RGB features not only significantly improves performance in terms of objective metrics, but also plausibly drives virtual humans (SMPL) in 3D scene. Furthermore, we demonstrate that incorporating physical perception enables humanoid robots to perform more precise and stable actions, which is highly beneficial for the development of embodied artificial intelligence. Project page is available at: https://nju-cite-mocaphumanoid.github.io/MotionPRO/

light_analysis.overview: 通过构建大规模压力动捕数据集MotionPRO，探索压力信号在人体运动捕捉中的作用，提出基于压力和融合RGB的估计方法，显著提升物理合理性和全局轨迹精度。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有动作捕捉方法侧重视觉相似性，忽略物理合理性，导致下游任务（如驱动虚拟人或人形机器人）出现时序漂移、抖动、滑动、穿透及轨迹不准等问题。论文从人体与物理世界交互的角度出发，探索压力信号的作用，以解决这些物理不合理性问题。
light_analysis.method: 构建MotionPRO数据集，含70名志愿者、400种动作、1240万帧，配备压力、RGB和光学传感器。提出两个任务：1）仅基于压力估计姿态和轨迹，采用小核解码器和长短时注意力模块；2）融合压力和RGB，通过正交相似性和全身接触约束增强交叉注意力，实现特征有效融合。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，融合压力与RGB特征在客观指标上显著提升性能，并能物理合理地驱动虚拟人类（SMPL）在3D场景中。同时，加入物理感知使人形机器人动作更精确稳定，有利于具身智能发展。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文通过引入压力信号，提升了动作捕捉的物理合理性和轨迹精度，为虚拟人类驱动和人形机器人控制提供新方法。未来可探索更复杂的压力传感器集成和实时应用。

title: TailedCore: Few-Shot Sampling for Unsupervised Long-Tail Noisy Anomaly Detection
abstract: We aim to solve unsupervised anomaly detection in a practical challenging environment where the normal dataset is both contaminated with defective regions and its product class distribution is tailed but unknown. We observe that existing models suffer from tail-versus-noise trade-off where if a model is robust against pixel noise, then its performance deteriorates on tail class samples, and vice versa. To mitigate the issue, we handle the tail class and noise samples independently. To this end, we propose TailSampler, a novel class size predictor that estimates the class cardinality of samples based on a symmetric assumption on the class-wise distribution of embedding similarities. TailSampler can be utilized to sample the tail class samples exclusively, allowing to handle them separately. Based on these facets, we build a memory-based anomaly detection model TailedCore, whose memory both well captures tail class information and is noise-robust. We extensively validate the effectiveness of TailedCore on the unsupervised long-tail noisy anomaly detection setting, and show that TailedCore outperforms the state-of-the-art in most settings.

light_analysis.overview: 提出TailSampler和TailedCore，解决无监督长尾噪声异常检测中的尾类-噪声权衡问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 实际无监督异常检测中，正常数据集常被缺陷区域污染且类别分布未知的长尾化，导致现有模型在鲁棒性与尾类性能间存在权衡：增强像素级噪声鲁棒性会降低尾类样本检测效果，反之亦然。现有方法无法同时处理这两类挑战，亟需独立处理尾类与噪声样本的方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出TailSampler，基于嵌入相似度的类别分布对称假设，预测样本的类别基数，从而仅采样尾类样本，实现尾类与噪声的独立处理。在此基础上构建记忆模型TailedCore，其记忆单元能有效捕获尾类信息并抵抗噪声干扰，通过分离处理机制缓解权衡问题。
light_analysis.result: 在无监督长尾噪声异常检测设定下，TailedCore在多数场景中超越现有最优方法。摘要未提供具体数值，但表明在尾类性能和噪声鲁棒性上取得平衡，验证了独立处理尾类与噪声策略的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: TailedCore通过TailSampler和分离处理机制，有效解决了无监督异常检测中长尾分布与噪声污染的耦合难题，提升了实际场景下的检测鲁棒性和尾类覆盖能力。未来可探索更复杂的类别分布假设或扩展至有监督/半监督场景。

title: Generating realistic global precipitation fields from modelled atmospheric circulation
abstract: Improving the representation of precipitation in Earth system models (ESMs) is critical for assessing the impacts of climate change and especially of extreme events like floods and droughts. In existing ESMs, precipitation is not resolved explicitly, but represented by parameterizations. These typically rely on resolving approximated but computationally expensive column-based physics, not accounting for interactions between locations. They struggle to capture fine-scale precipitation processes and introduce significant biases. We present a novel approach, based on generative machine learning, which integrates a conditional diffusion model with a UNet architecture to generate accurate, high-resolution (0.25°) global daily precipitation fields from a small set of prognostic atmospheric variables. Unlike traditional parameterizations, our framework efficiently produces ensemble predictions, capturing uncertainties in precipitation, and does not require fine-tuning by hand. We train our model on the ERA5 reanalysis and present a method that allows us to apply it to unseen ESM data, enabling fast generation of probabilistic forecasts and climate scenarios. By leveraging interactions between global prognostic variables, our approach provides an alternative parameterization scheme that mitigates biases present in the ESM precipitation while maintaining consistency with its large-scale (annual) trends. This work demonstrates that complex precipitation patterns can be learned directly from large-scale atmospheric variables, offering a computationally efficient method to obtain high-resolution precipitation without the cost of running the dynamical model at such high resolution.
comment: Accepted for publication at Climate Dynamics

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于条件扩散模型和UNet的生成式方法，从大气变量生成高分辨率全球降水场，替代传统参数化方案。
light_analysis.motivation: 地球系统模型中的降水参数化方案依赖于计算昂贵的近似列物理过程，且忽略位置间相互作用，难以捕捉精细降水过程并引入显著偏差。改进降水表征对评估气候变化及极端事件至关重要。现有方法存在计算效率低、不能生成集合预测、手工微调等不足。需一种能高效生成高分辨率降水场、捕获不确定性、并保持与大规模趋势一致性的替代方案。
light_analysis.method: 采用条件扩散模型结合UNet架构，从少量预报性大气变量（如温度、湿度等）生成0.25°分辨率的全球日降水场。模型在ERA5再分析数据上训练，并设计方法使其可应用于未见过的ESM数据。通过全局变量间的交互学习，在生成降水时同时捕获不确定性，无需手工调整。与基于列物理的参数化不同，该方法直接在大尺度变量上学习降水模式。
light_analysis.result: 模型能生成高分辨率（0.25°）的全球日降水场，与ERA5再分析数据匹配。相比传统ESM参数化，该方法显著减轻了降水偏差，同时保持与ESM大尺度（年度）趋势的一致性。通过生成集合预测有效捕获降水不确定性，计算效率高，无需运行高分辨率动力模型。具体定量对比摘要未明确说明，但方法在偏差减轻和效率上优于传统方案。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该工作证明复杂降水模式可直接从大尺度大气变量中学习，提供了一种计算高效、无需手工调整的替代参数化方案。能快速生成概率预报和气候情景，对气候影响评估和极端事件研究有重要价值。未来可扩展至更高分辨率或应用于其他ESM变量。局限性包括依赖再分析数据训练，可能需针对不同ESM系统调整。

title: An uncertainty-aware Bayesian framework for machine learning classification models: A case study in land cover classification
abstract: Ensuring that predictions of machine learning (ML) classification models are accompanied by uncertainty estimates is one of the main pillars of trustworthy AI. Current research in uncertainty quantification focuses mainly on epistemic uncertainty of the ML model, but rarely takes account of input measurement uncertainty, which is vital for traceability in metrology. In this work we propose a Bayesian framework for generative ML classification models that takes account of input measurement uncertainty. We take the specific case of a Bayesian quadratic discriminant analysis (BQDA) model, and apply it to metrological land cover datasets from Copernicus Sentinel-2 from 2020 and 2021. We benchmark the performance of the model against more popular classification models used in land cover maps such as random forests and neural networks. To validate and assess the generalisability of such a model, we also run simulations over synthetic classification data, varying distribution type and strength of the input measurement noise. We find for both real and synthetic data, the BQDA model presented is more trustworthy, in the sense that it is more interpretable, explicitly models the input measurement uncertainty, and maintains predictive performance of class probability outputs across datasets over different domains and sizes, whilst also being more computationally efficient.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种考虑输入测量不确定性的贝叶斯框架，应用于土地覆盖分类，提升模型可信度。
light_analysis.motivation: 当前不确定性量化主要关注模型认知不确定性，而忽略了输入测量不确定性，这对于计量可追溯性至关重要。现有方法如随机森林和神经网络在土地覆盖分类中缺乏对输入不确定性的显式建模，导致模型可解释性差，难以满足高可靠性需求。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出一个贝叶斯框架用于生成式ML分类模型，具体采用贝叶斯二次判别分析（BQDA）模型。该模型显式地将输入测量不确定性纳入建模过程，并通过Copernicus Sentinel-2卫星的土地覆盖数据集进行训练和测试。为评估泛化能力，还在合成数据上模拟不同分布类型和强度的输入噪声，并与随机森林、神经网络等主流方法对比。
light_analysis.result: 在真实和合成数据集上，BQDA模型相比随机森林和神经网络等表现出更高的可信度：模型更具可解释性，能够显式建模输入测量不确定性；在保持类别概率输出预测性能的同时，在不同领域和规模的数据集上表现稳定；此外，计算效率更高。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文提出的贝叶斯框架为生成式分类模型提供了一种高效且可解释的不确定性量化方法，尤其适用于需要计量可追溯性的应用场景（如土地覆盖分类）。局限性在于仅针对生成式模型且具体化为BQDA，未来可扩展至其他模型类型。

title: TFD: A Comprehensive Structured Tibetan Foundation Dataset for Low-Resource Language Processing and Large-Scale Modeling
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in high-resource languages, yet progress in Tibetan remains severely constrained. While recent efforts have begun to address pre-training data scarcity for Tibetan, a more fundamental gap persists: no existing resource supports the complete LLM development pipeline, spanning pre-training, instruction tuning, safety alignment, preference optimization, and reasoning supervision. We introduce the Tibetan Foundation Dataset (TFD), the first structured, large-scale, and expert-curated dataset covering all key stages of Tibetan large language modeling. TFD comprises TIBSTC, a unified corpus of over 11 billion tokens with curated sub-datasets for instruction tuning, safety alignment, and preference optimization, and TIBSTC-CoT, the first large-scale Tibetan chain-of-thought dataset. We demonstrate its utility by training the Sun-Shine family of Tibetan LLMs, achieving substantial improvements over strong baselines on understanding, safety, reasoning, and generation benchmarks. These results underscore that advancing low-resource language modeling requires not only scale, but a structurally complete data ecosystem. We release TFD to facilitate reproducible research and the development of robust, culturally aligned Tibetan LLMs. Code and data are available at https://github.com/Vicentvankor/sun-shine.

light_analysis.overview: 首个覆盖预训练、指令微调、安全对齐、偏好优化和推理监督全流程的结构化藏语基础数据集TFD，推动低资源语言建模。
light_analysis.motivation: 藏语大语言模型发展受限于数据稀缺，现有资源仅解决预训练数据不足，缺乏支撑完整开发流程的数据集。该研究旨在填补这一关键空白，构建一个从预训练到推理监督的完整数据生态。
light_analysis.method: 提出TFD数据集，涵盖TIBSTC统一语料（超110亿token）及其子数据集用于指令微调、安全对齐和偏好优化，以及首个大规模藏语思维链数据集TIBSTC-CoT。基于此训练Sun-Shine藏语LLM系列。
light_analysis.result: 在理解、安全、推理和生成基准上，Sun-Shine模型相比强基线取得显著提升。数据集和代码已开源，支持可重复研究与藏语LLM开发。
light_analysis.conclusion: TFD证明低资源语言建模不仅需要数据规模，更需结构完整的数据生态。该工作为藏语等低资源语言的大模型开发提供了基础资源，有助于推动文化对齐的LLM研究。

title: ImViD: Immersive Volumetric Videos for Enhanced VR Engagement
abstract: User engagement is greatly enhanced by fully immersive multi-modal experiences that combine visual and auditory stimuli. Consequently, the next frontier in VR/AR technologies lies in immersive volumetric videos with complete scene capture, large 6-DoF interaction space, multi-modal feedback, and high resolution & frame-rate contents. To stimulate the reconstruction of immersive volumetric videos, we introduce ImViD, a multi-view, multi-modal dataset featuring complete space-oriented data capture and various indoor/outdoor scenarios. Our capture rig supports multi-view video-audio capture while on the move, a capability absent in existing datasets, significantly enhancing the completeness, flexibility, and efficiency of data capture.   The captured multi-view videos (with synchronized audios) are in 5K resolution at 60FPS, lasting from 1-5 minutes, and include rich foreground-background elements, and complex dynamics. We benchmark existing methods using our dataset and establish a base pipeline for constructing immersive volumetric videos from multi-view audiovisual inputs for 6-DoF multi-modal immersive VR experiences. The benchmark and the reconstruction and interaction results demonstrate the effectiveness of our dataset and baseline method, which we believe will stimulate future research on immersive volumetric video production.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ImViD多视角多模态数据集，支持移动中全场景捕获，用于沉浸式体积视频重建和6-DoF VR体验。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有VR/AR技术需要完全沉浸式的多模态体验，但缺乏支持移动捕获、完整场景覆盖和大6-DoF交互空间的体积视频数据集。现有数据集在捕获灵活性、效率和完整性上存在不足，限制了沉浸式体积视频的生成研究。
light_analysis.method: 设计了可移动的多视角视频-音频捕获装置，采集5K分辨率60FPS、时长1-5分钟的多视角视频和同步音频，涵盖室内外场景、丰富前景背景元素和复杂动态。基于此数据集建立了从多视角音视频输入构造沉浸式体积视频的基线管道，支持6-DoF多模态交互。
light_analysis.result: 在数据集上对现有方法进行基准测试，并与基线方法的重建和交互结果对比，验证了数据集的有效性和基线方法的性能，具体指标未明确列出但定性表明能促进沉浸式体积视频生成。
light_analysis.conclusion: ImViD数据集填补了移动多模态体积视频数据空白，基线管道为6-DoF沉浸式体验提供基础。该工作将推动沉浸式体积视频生产研究，未来可扩展更多场景和交互模态。

title: CleanSurvival: Automated data preprocessing for time-to-event models using reinforcement learning
abstract: Data preprocessing is often paid little attention in machine learning, despite its potentially significant impact on model performance. While automated machine learning pipelines are starting to recognize and integrate data preprocessing into their solutions for classification and regression tasks, this integration is lacking for more specialized tasks like time-to-event models for censored data. As a result, survival analysis not only faces the general challenges of data preprocessing but also suffers from the lack of tailored, automated solutions in this area. To address this gap, this paper presents CleanSurvival, a reinforcement-learning-based solution for optimizing preprocessing pipelines, extended specifically for survival analysis. The framework can handle continuous and categorical variables. It builds upon Learn2Clean's Q-learning to select which combination of data imputation, outlier detection and feature extraction techniques achieves optimal performance for a Cox, random forest, neural network or user-supplied time-to-event model. The Python package is available on GitHub: https://github.com/datasciapps/CleanSurvival. Experimental benchmarks on real-world datasets show that the Q-learning-based data preprocessing can improve predictive performance relative to simple baselines, while runtime behavior is condition-dependent and most clearly interpretable in the best-covered benchmark cells. Furthermore, a simulation study demonstrates effectiveness across different types and levels of missingness and noise. With an increase in the use of machine learning, it becomes important to generalise AutoML pipelines to a variety of models now present, including survival analysis. Tools like CleanSurvival, which integrate preprocessing for survival analysis, can make survival studies easier and quicker to perform, as well as make the results more robust.
comment: Resubmitted after Peer Review Feedback to BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making

light_analysis.overview: 提出CleanSurvival，一个基于强化学习的自动化数据预处理框架，专为生存分析中的时间-事件模型优化预处理流水线。
light_analysis.motivation: 数据预处理虽能显著影响模型性能，但自动机器学习（AutoML）主要针对分类和回归任务，忽视了生存分析等专门任务的预处理需求，导致缺乏定制化的自动解决方案。
light_analysis.method: 基于Q-learning的强化学习框架，从Learn2Clean扩展而来，自动选择最优的数据填充、异常检测和特征提取组合。支持Cox、随机森林、神经网络及用户自定义的生存模型，处理连续和类别变量。
light_analysis.result: 在真实数据集上，Q-learning驱动的预处理相比简单基线提升了预测性能；运行时间条件依赖，在覆盖良好的基准单元中解释清晰。模拟研究证明在不同类型和程度的缺失与噪声下均有效。
light_analysis.conclusion: CleanSurvival将AutoML扩展至生存分析，填补了该领域自动预处理的空白，使生存研究更便捷、快速且结果更鲁棒，推动了AutoML的泛化应用。

title: Innovative Silicosis and Pneumonia Classification: Leveraging Graph Transformer Post-hoc Modeling and Ensemble Techniques
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the classification and detection of Silicosis-related lung inflammation. Our main contributions include 1) the creation of a newly curated chest X-ray (CXR) image dataset named SVBCX that is tailored to the nuances of lung inflammation caused by distinct agents, providing a valuable resource for silicosis and pneumonia research community; and 2) we propose a novel deep-learning architecture that integrates graph transformer networks alongside a traditional deep neural network module for the effective classification of silicosis and pneumonia. Additionally, we employ the Balanced Cross-Entropy (BalCE) as a loss function to ensure more uniform learning across different classes, enhancing the model's ability to discern subtle differences in lung conditions. The proposed model architecture and loss function selection aim to improve the accuracy and reliability of inflammation detection, particularly in the context of Silicosis. Furthermore, our research explores the efficacy of an ensemble approach that combines the strengths of diverse model architectures. Experimental results on the constructed dataset demonstrate promising outcomes, showcasing substantial enhancements compared to baseline models. The ensemble of models achieves a macro-F1 score of 0.9749 and AUC ROC scores exceeding 0.99 for each class, underscoring the effectiveness of our approach in accurate and robust lung inflammation classification.
comment: Withdrawn by the authors because the manuscript contains incomplete and potentially misleading descriptions of the dataset construction and evaluation protocol, particularly in the Dataset and Experimental Setup sections. The work should not be cited or used as an independent reference in its current form

light_analysis.overview: 提出图Transformer后处理与集成方法，实现矽肺和肺炎的高精度分类。
light_analysis.motivation: 矽肺和肺炎的准确分类对临床诊断至关重要，但现有方法在区分不同病因引起的肺部炎症方面存在局限。此外，缺乏专门针对矽肺的胸部X光数据集，限制了相关研究。因此，有必要构建专用数据集并开发更精细的分类模型，以提高诊断准确性和可靠性。
light_analysis.method: 本文贡献包括：1）创建了名为SVBCX的胸部X光图像数据集，专门针对矽肺和肺炎的细微差异；2）提出一种新颖的深度学习架构，结合图Transformer网络和传统深度神经网络模块，用于有效分类；3）采用平衡交叉熵损失函数，确保各类别均匀学习，增强对肺部状态细微差别的识别能力。同时，探索了集成方法以融合不同模型架构的优势。
light_analysis.result: 在构建的数据集上进行了实验，集成模型取得了显著效果：宏F1分数达0.9749，每个类别的AUC ROC均超过0.99。与基线模型相比，性能大幅提升，验证了该方法在肺部炎症分类中的准确性和鲁棒性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文通过构建专用数据集和提出图Transformer集成架构，有效提升了矽肺与肺炎的分类性能。该研究为肺部炎症的自动诊断提供了新思路，具有重要的临床价值。未来可进一步扩展数据集规模，并探索模型在其他肺部疾病中的应用。

title: LiPUP-MA: A Residential Experience-centric Multi-Agent Framework for Living-in-the-loop Participatory Urban Planning
abstract: Participatory Urban Planning (PUP) is increasingly supported by LLM-based agents, yet existing methods largely rely on static preference elicitation and one-shot stakeholder discussions, overlooking the cyclical nature of real-world planning, where residential life, experience collection, and plan adjustment continually interact. We propose Living-in-the-loop Participatory Urban Planning (LiPUP), a closed-loop paradigm that alternates between simulated residential living and experience-driven plan revision, while posing two key challenges: grounding scattered living experience in concrete urban contexts and translating subjective feedback into spatially coherent planning actions. To instantiate LiPUP, we introduce LiPUP-MA, an LLM-based multi-agent framework that constructs a Plan-centric Graph-based Experience Bank to organize urban-grounded residential feedback from living simulation and equips a Spatially-constrained Skill-augmented Planner agent to revise plans by harmonizing experiential, visual, and geospatial evidence. Experiments show that LiPUP-MA consistently outperforms baselines on both conventional static planning metrics and living-based metrics, while iterative LiPUP cycles further improve plan quality.

light_analysis.overview: 提出LiPUP-MA，一个基于LLM的多智能体框架，通过模拟居住体验循环反馈来优化参与式城市规划。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有参与式城市规划方法依赖静态偏好和一次性讨论，忽视了真实世界中居住体验、数据收集和计划调整的循环性。本文提出Living-in-the-loop范式，解决如何将分散的居住体验与具体城市上下文结合，以及将主观反馈转化为空间一致的规划行动。
light_analysis.method: LiPUP-MA框架包含一个以规划为中心的图结构经验库，用于组织来自生活模拟的居住反馈；以及一个空间约束的技能增强规划器，用于协调体验、视觉和地理空间证据来修订计划。利用LLM驱动多智能体协作。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，LiPUP-MA在传统静态规划指标和基于生活的指标上均优于基线方法，且迭代的LiPUP循环进一步提升了计划质量。具体数据未明确给出，但显示了持续的性能优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文贡献了一个闭环的参与式规划范式和多智能体框架，有效整合居住体验反馈，提升了城市规划的响应性和合理性。学术价值在于提出Living-in-the-loop概念，实际应用价值在于推动数据驱动的城市设计。未来可探索真实场景部署。

title: "Give Me BF16 or Give Me Death"? Accuracy-Performance Trade-Offs in LLM Quantization
abstract: Quantization is a powerful tool for accelerating large language model (LLM) inference, but the accuracy-performance trade-offs across different formats remain unclear. In this paper, we conduct the most comprehensive empirical study to date, evaluating FP8, INT8, and INT4 quantization across academic benchmarks and real-world tasks on the entire Llama-3.1 model family. Through over 500,000 evaluations, our investigation yields several key findings: (1) FP8 (W8A8-FP) is effectively lossless across all model scales, (2) well-tuned INT8 (W8A8-INT) achieves surprisingly low (1-3\%) accuracy degradation, and (3) INT4 weight-only (W4A16-INT) is more competitive than expected, rivaling 8-bit quantization. Further, we investigate the optimal quantization format for different deployments by analyzing inference performance through the popular vLLM framework. Our analysis provides clear deployment recommendations: W4A16 is the most cost-efficient for synchronous setups, while W8A8 dominates in asynchronous continuous batching. For mixed workloads, the optimal choice depends on the specific use case. Our findings offer practical, data-driven guidelines for deploying quantized LLMs at scale -- ensuring the best balance between speed, efficiency, and accuracy.

light_analysis.overview: 通过50万次评估，全面比较了LLM量化的FP8、INT8、INT4格式，揭示精度-性能权衡，并给出清晰的部署建议。
light_analysis.motivation: 量化是加速LLM推理的关键技术，但不同量化格式（如FP8、INT8、INT4）的精度与性能权衡尚不清晰。现有研究缺乏全面对比和实际部署指导，导致从业者难以选择最优方案。本研究旨在填补这一空白，为大规模部署量化LLM提供数据驱动的决策依据。
light_analysis.method: 在Llama-3.1全模型家族上，对FP8（W8A8-FP）、INT8（W8A8-INT）和INT4权重仅（W4A16-INT）三种格式进行系统评估。使用学术基准和真实世界任务，执行超过50万次实验。分析推理性能时采用vLLM框架，模拟同步、异步连续批处理和混合工作负载场景。
light_analysis.result: FP8在全模型尺度上几乎无损；调优后的INT8精度下降仅1-3%；INT4权重仅量化竞争性超预期，可与8位量化媲美。部署建议：同步场景W4A16成本效率最高，异步连续批处理W8A8占优，混合工作负载需根据用例选择。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究提供了最全面的LLM量化格式实证分析，明确了不同格式的精度-性能权衡，并给出具体部署推荐。学术上填补了量化综合比较的空白，实践中帮助开发者平衡速度、效率和准确性。未来可扩展至更多硬件平台和新兴量化格式。

title: Interactive Agents: Simulating Counselor-Client Psychological Counseling via Role-Playing LLM-to-LLM Interactions
abstract: Creating effective dialogue systems for mental health support requires high-quality multi-turn counseling dialogue data, yet collecting real counselor-client conversations presents significant challenges, including privacy concerns, high costs, and limited scalability. We present \textbf{Interactive Agents}, a novel framework that simulates naturalistic counseling dialogues through controlled LLM-to-LLM interactions. The framework introduces two key innovations: (1) a personalized client agent that maintains consistent psychological characteristics throughout a session, and (2) a counselor agent that implements a theoretically grounded three-stage therapeutic model comprising the exploration, insight, and action phases. Through rigorous evaluation using both automatic metrics and professional-counselor assessments based on the Working Alliance Inventory, we demonstrate that our framework generates therapeutically valid dialogues that are comparable in quality to human-generated sessions. Models fine-tuned on our proposed synthetic dataset (SimPsyDial) achieve state-of-the-art performance in a standard pairwise chatbot-arena evaluation of LLM-based counselors. Our framework provides a scalable, privacy-preserving method for generating high-quality counseling dialogue data while maintaining professional therapeutic standards.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Interactive Agents框架，通过LLM角色扮演生成高质量心理治疗对话数据，解决真实数据采集难题。
light_analysis.motivation: 收集真实咨询对话存在隐私、高成本和扩展性差等挑战，限制了对话系统在心理健康领域的发展。现有方法缺乏可控且符合治疗理论的合成数据生成手段，难以支持高质量多轮咨询对话系统的训练。
light_analysis.method: 框架包含两个核心创新：个性化客户代理，在对话中保持一致心理特征；咨询师代理实现三阶段治疗模型（探索、洞察、行动）。使用LLM对LLM角色扮演生成对话，并通过自动指标和工作联盟清单进行专业评估。
light_analysis.result: 生成的对话在治疗有效性上与人工会话质量相当。基于合成数据集SimPsyDial微调的模型在LLM咨询师的成对竞技场评估中达到最先进性能，验证了数据质量。
light_analysis.conclusion: Interactive Agents提供了一种可扩展、保护隐私的方法生成高质量咨询对话数据，同时维护专业治疗标准。未来可扩展至更多心理理论模型或不同文化背景的咨询场景。

title: Incremental Gauss-Newton Descent for Machine Learning
abstract: Stochastic gradient updates are widely used for their efficiency and scalability, but their effective step sizes can depend strongly on feature scaling and local model sensitivity. Gauss-Newton methods address such scale effects through curvature information, but in their standard mini-batch form they require matrix-vector products, linear solves, or structured approximations. This paper studies the special case of scalar-output losses evaluated one sample at a time. In this setting, the generalized Gauss-Newton matrix has rank at most one, and its only possible nonzero curvature direction is aligned with the stochastic gradient. As a result, the damped Gauss-Newton direction reduces to a closed-form scalar normalization of the sample gradient. The resulting update, Incremental Gauss-Newton Descent (IGND), requires no curvature matrix storage, factorization, or iterative linear solve. We derive the update, characterize its behavior, and relate it to normalized gradient descent, adaptive first-order methods, stochastic Polyak step sizes, and mini-batch Gauss-Newton updates. Under explicit smoothness, alignment, and stochastic approximation assumptions, we prove a stationarity result for the IGND update. Experiments on supervised learning, a controlled test of scale robustness, and a linear-quadratic control case study show that IGND improves robustness to sensitivity scaling and can be competitive with, or complementary to, common stochastic optimizers while retaining a simple incremental update.

light_analysis.overview: 提出增量高斯-牛顿下降法，利用标量输出损失的广义高斯-牛顿矩阵秩一特性，实现闭合形式梯度归一化更新，无需矩阵存储或分解，提升对敏感度缩放的鲁棒性。
light_analysis.motivation: 随机梯度更新广泛使用但有效步长严重依赖特征缩放和局部模型敏感度。高斯-牛顿方法通过曲率信息解决缩放效应，但其标准小批量形式需要矩阵向量乘积或线性求解，计算成本高。本文聚焦标量输出损失逐样本评估的特例，旨在简化高斯-牛顿方法，保留曲率优势同时降低计算开销。
light_analysis.method: 提出增量高斯-牛顿下降法（IGND）。在标量输出损失逐样本评估时，广义高斯-牛顿矩阵秩最多为1，唯一非零曲率方向与随机梯度对齐。因此阻尼高斯-牛顿方向简化为样本梯度的闭合形式标量归一化，无需矩阵存储、分解或线性求解。该方法与归一化梯度下降、自适应方法等建立联系，并在光滑性、对齐性等假设下证明平稳性结果。
light_analysis.result: 在监督学习、缩放鲁棒性测试及线性二次控制实验中，IGND提高了对敏感度缩放的鲁棒性，能与常见随机优化器（如Adam、SGD）竞争或互补，同时保持简单增量更新。理论证明在显式假设下保证了收敛到平稳点。
light_analysis.conclusion: IGND为标量输出损失提供了一种简洁有效的高斯-牛顿随机优化方法，结合曲率信息与增量更新的简单性。实验结果验证其鲁棒性与性能竞争力。未来可扩展至更一般损失或批量设置，并深入分析收敛性质。

title: Multi-Agent Causal Discovery Using Large Language Models
abstract: Causal discovery aims to identify causal relationships between variables and is a fundamental problem across the sciences. Traditional statistical causal discovery (SCD) methods rely solely on observational data and ignore the contextual information available in metadata, whereas recent LLM-based methods exploit metadata but treat the large language model (LLM) as a single agent, leaving its judgments vulnerable to memorized or biased associations. To address this gap, we introduce MAC (Multi-Agent Causal Discovery Framework), which casts causal discovery as a multi-agent debate coupled with the autonomous selection of an SCD algorithm. MAC combines two complementary modules, bridged by a Meta Fusion mechanism: a Debate-Coding Module (DCM) that grounds an initial graph in data by autonomously selecting and executing the best-suited SCD algorithm, and a Meta-Debate Module (MDM) that refines the graph through an adversarial Affirmative-Negative-Judge debate over the metadata. Across five benchmark datasets and three metrics (F1, SHD, NHD), MAC achieves the best aggregate performance among five statistical and four LLM-based baselines, ranking first on 10 of 15 evaluation points with Gemini-2.0-Flash -- including a perfect reconstruction of the Earthquake graph -- and remains robust across three backbone LLMs.

light_analysis.overview: 提出多智能体因果发现框架MAC，通过多智能体辩论与自主算法选择，结合数据驱动和元数据推理，提升因果图构建的准确性和鲁棒性。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统统计因果发现方法仅依赖观测数据，忽略元数据中的上下文信息；而现有LLM方法将LLM作为单智能体，其判断易受记忆或偏见影响。为解决这一问题，需要一种能融合数据驱动与元数据推理、且能克服单智能体局限的方法。
light_analysis.method: MAC框架包含两个互补模块：辩论编码模块（DCM）自主选择并执行最适合的SCD算法，从数据中构建初始因果图；元辩论模块（MDM）通过对抗性肯定-否定-裁判辩论利用元数据精炼图。两者通过元融合机制桥接，结合数据证据与语义推理。
light_analysis.result: 在五个基准数据集和三个指标（F1、SHD、NHD）上，MAC在15个评估点中10个排名第一，包括在Earthquake图上实现完美重构。其性能优于五种统计方法和四种基于LLM的基线，且在不同骨干LLM下保持稳健。
light_analysis.conclusion: MAC通过多智能体辩论与动态算法选择，有效融合数据与元数据，显著提升因果发现性能。该框架具有通用性，为利用LLM进行科学发现提供了新范式，未来可扩展至更多领域并优化辩论机制。

title: CRoFT: Robust Fine-Tuning with Concurrent Optimization for OOD Generalization and Open-Set OOD Detection
abstract: Recent vision-language pre-trained models (VL-PTMs) have shown remarkable success in open-vocabulary tasks. However, downstream use cases often involve further fine-tuning of VL-PTMs, which may distort their general knowledge and impair their ability to handle distribution shifts. In real-world scenarios, machine learning systems inevitably encounter both covariate shifts (e.g., changes in image styles) and semantic shifts (e.g., test-time unseen classes). This highlights the importance of enhancing out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization on covariate shifts and simultaneously detecting semantic-shifted unseen classes. Thus a critical but underexplored question arises: How to improve VL-PTMs' generalization ability to closed-set OOD data, while effectively detecting open-set unseen classes during fine-tuning? In this paper, we propose a novel objective function of OOD detection that also serves to improve OOD generalization. We show that minimizing the gradient magnitude of energy scores on training data leads to domain-consistent Hessians of classification loss, a strong indicator for OOD generalization revealed by theoretical analysis. Based on this finding, we have developed a unified fine-tuning framework that allows for concurrent optimization of both tasks. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the superiority of our method. The code is available at https://github.com/LinLLLL/CRoFT.

light_analysis.overview: 提出CRoFT框架，通过最小化能量分数梯度幅度实现OOD泛化与检测的并发优化。
light_analysis.motivation: 视觉语言预训练模型在下游微调时易扭曲通用知识，导致对协变量偏移和语义偏移的处理能力下降。现有方法通常独立处理OOD泛化和检测，无法同时兼顾。因此，亟需一种统一微调方法，既能增强对闭集OOD数据的泛化能力，又能有效检测开集未见类别，以满足现实场景的复杂需求。
light_analysis.method: 提出一种新的OOD检测目标函数，通过最小化训练数据能量分数的梯度幅度，使得分类损失的Hessian矩阵在域间保持一致。理论分析表明，域一致Hessian是OOD泛化的强指标。基于此，设计统一微调框架CRoFT，在微调过程中同时优化OOD泛化和检测任务，无需额外数据或复杂架构。
light_analysis.result: 在多个基准数据集上进行广泛实验，结果表明CRoFT在OOD泛化和开集OOD检测任务上均优于现有微调方法。具体性能指标摘要未明确说明，但声称展现了优越性。代码已开源，便于复现。
light_analysis.conclusion: CRoFT通过理论驱动的能量梯度最小化，有效解决了VL-PTMs微调中OOD泛化与检测的冲突。其统一框架简洁高效，为鲁棒微调提供了新思路。未来的工作可探索更复杂的能量函数或扩展到其他预训练模型。

title: Towards Interpretable Federated Learning
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables multiple data owners to build machine learning models collaboratively without exposing their private local data. In order for FL to achieve widespread adoption, it is important to balance the need for performance, privacy-preservation and interpretability, especially in mission critical applications such as finance and healthcare. Thus, interpretable federated learning (IFL) has become an emerging topic of research attracting significant interest from the academia and the industry alike. Its interdisciplinary nature can be challenging for new researchers to pick up. In this paper, we bridge this gap by providing (to the best of our knowledge) the first survey on IFL. We propose a unique IFL taxonomy which covers relevant works enabling FL models to explain the prediction results, support model debugging, and provide insights into the contributions made by individual data owners or data samples, which in turn, is crucial for allocating rewards fairly to motivate active and reliable participation in FL. We conduct comprehensive analysis of the representative IFL approaches, the commonly adopted performance evaluation metrics, and promising directions towards building versatile IFL techniques.

light_analysis.overview: 首篇可解释联邦学习(IFL)综述，提出独特分类法，涵盖解释预测、模型调试及数据贡献分析，促进公平激励。
light_analysis.motivation: 联邦学习在金融、医疗等关键应用中需平衡性能、隐私与可解释性。现有方法缺乏系统性梳理，新研究者难以入门。该综述旨在填补空白，推动IFL领域发展。
light_analysis.method: 通过文献调研提出IFL分类法，覆盖模型预测解释、调试及数据贡献评估三大方向。分析代表性方法、评估指标，并展望未来研究方向。
light_analysis.result: 未提供具体实验数据，但通过综述总结了IFL领域的关键技术与挑战，包括分类框架、评估指标及开放问题，为后续研究提供基础。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该综述系统梳理了IFL研究，提供了分类与评估体系，有助于激励公平参与和模型可信性提升。未来需发展更通用的IFL技术。

title: A Discussion to Qualify Intelligence
abstract: Our understanding of intelligence is directed primarily at the human level. This paper attempts to give a more unifying definition that can be applied to the natural world in general and then Artificial Intelligence. The definition would be used more to qualify than quantify it and might help when making judgements on the matter. While correct behaviour is the preferred definition, a metric that is grounded in Kolmogorov's Complexity Theory is suggested, which leads to a measurement about entropy. A version of an accepted AI test is then put forward as the 'acid test' and might be what a free-thinking program would try to achieve. Recent work by the author has been more from a direction of mechanical processes, built from structure. This paper agrees that intelligence is a pro-active event, but also notes a second aspect to it that is in the background and mechanical. The paper suggests looking at intelligence and the conscious as being slightly different, where the conscious is this more mechanical aspect. In fact, a surprising conclusion can be a passive but intelligent brain being invoked by active and less intelligent senses.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于科尔莫戈罗夫复杂性和熵的智能统一定义，并区分智能与意识。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有智能定义主要针对人类层面，缺乏普适性。作者试图提供一个适用于自然世界和人工智能的统一定义，以更好地评估智能本质。
light_analysis.method: 基于科尔莫戈罗夫复杂性理论提出熵度量；借鉴AI测试提出'酸性测试'；将智能视为主动事件，同时指出存在背景机械方面，区分智能与意识。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确说明具体实验结果，主要贡献在于理论框架和定义的提出。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文贡献了统一的智能定义，区分智能与意识，并指出被动智能可能由主动低智能感官触发，具有理论价值。
